constraints.title: ACP
constraints.description: ACP runtime controls for enabling dispatch, selecting backends, constraining allowed agent targets, and tuning streamed turn projection behavior.

constraints.title: ACP Allowed Agents
constraints.description: Allowlist of ACP target agent ids permitted for ACP runtime sessions. Empty means no additional allowlist restriction.

constraints.title: ACP Backend
constraints.description: Default ACP runtime backend id (for example: acpx). Must match a registered ACP runtime plugin backend.

constraints.title: ACP Default Agent
constraints.description: Fallback ACP target agent id used when ACP spawns do not specify an explicit target.

constraints.title: ACP Dispatch Enabled
constraints.description: Independent dispatch gate for ACP session turns (default: true). Set false to keep ACP commands available while blocking ACP turn execution.

constraints.title: ACP Enabled
constraints.description: Global ACP feature gate. Keep disabled unless ACP runtime + policy are configured.

constraints.title: ACP Max Concurrent Sessions
constraints.description: Maximum concurrently active ACP sessions across this gateway process.

constraints.title: ACP Runtime Install Command
constraints.description: Optional operator install/setup command shown by `/acp install` and `/acp doctor` when ACP backend wiring is missing.

constraints.title: ACP Runtime TTL (minutes)
constraints.description: Idle runtime TTL in minutes for ACP session workers before eligible cleanup.

constraints.title: ACP Stream
constraints.description: ACP streaming projection controls for chunk sizing, metadata visibility, and deduped delivery behavior.

constraints.title: ACP Stream Coalesce Idle (ms)
constraints.description: Coalescer idle flush window in milliseconds for ACP streamed text before block replies are emitted.

constraints.title: ACP Stream Delivery Mode
constraints.description: ACP delivery style: live streams projected output incrementally, final_only buffers all projected ACP output until terminal turn events.

constraints.title: ACP Stream Hidden Boundary Separator
constraints.description: Separator inserted before next visible assistant text when hidden ACP tool lifecycle events occurred (none|space|newline|paragraph). Default: paragraph.

constraints.title: ACP Stream Max Chunk Chars
constraints.description: Maximum chunk size for ACP streamed block projection before splitting into multiple block replies.

constraints.title: ACP Stream Max Output Chars
constraints.description: Maximum assistant output characters projected per ACP turn before truncation notice is emitted.

constraints.title: ACP Stream Max Session Update Chars
constraints.description: Maximum characters for projected ACP session/update lines (tool/status updates).

constraints.title: ACP Stream Repeat Suppression
constraints.description: When true (default), suppress repeated ACP status/tool projection lines in a turn while keeping raw ACP events unchanged.

constraints.title: ACP Stream Tag Visibility
constraints.description: Per-sessionUpdate visibility overrides for ACP projection (for example usage_update, available_commands_update).

constraints.title: Agents
constraints.description: Agent runtime configuration root covering defaults and explicit agent entries used for routing and execution context. Keep this section explicit so model/tool behavior stays predictable across multi-agent workflows.

constraints.title: Agent Defaults
constraints.description: Shared default settings inherited by agents unless overridden per entry in agents.list. Use defaults to enforce consistent baseline behavior and reduce duplicated per-agent configuration.

constraints.title: Bootstrap Max Chars
constraints.description: Max characters of each workspace bootstrap file injected into the system prompt before truncation (default: 20000).

constraints.title: Bootstrap Prompt Truncation Warning
constraints.description: Inject agent-visible warning text when bootstrap files are truncated: "off", "once" (default), or "always".

constraints.title: Bootstrap Total Max Chars
constraints.description: Max total characters across all injected workspace bootstrap files (default: 150000).

constraints.title: CLI Backends
constraints.description: Optional CLI backends for text-only fallback (claude-cli, etc.).

constraints.title: Compaction
constraints.description: Compaction tuning for when context nears token limits, including history share, reserve headroom, and pre-compaction memory flush behavior. Use this when long-running sessions need stable continuity under tight context windows.

constraints.title: Compaction Identifier Instructions
constraints.description: Custom identifier-preservation instruction text used when identifierPolicy="custom". Keep this explicit and safety-focused so compaction summaries do not rewrite opaque IDs, URLs, hosts, or ports.

constraints.title: Compaction Identifier Policy
constraints.description: Identifier-preservation policy for compaction summaries: "strict" prepends built-in opaque-identifier retention guidance (default), "off" disables this prefix, and "custom" uses identifierInstructions. Keep "strict" unless you have a specific compatibility need.

constraints.title: Compaction Keep Recent Tokens
constraints.description: Minimum token budget preserved from the most recent conversation window during compaction. Use higher values to protect immediate context continuity and lower values to keep more long-tail history.

constraints.title: Compaction Max History Share
constraints.description: Maximum fraction of total context budget allowed for retained history after compaction (range 0.1-0.9). Use lower shares for more generation headroom or higher shares for deeper historical continuity.

constraints.title: Compaction Memory Flush
constraints.description: Pre-compaction memory flush settings that run an agentic memory write before heavy compaction. Keep enabled for long sessions so salient context is persisted before aggressive trimming.

constraints.title: Compaction Memory Flush Enabled
constraints.description: Enables pre-compaction memory flush before the runtime performs stronger history reduction near token limits. Keep enabled unless you intentionally disable memory side effects in constrained environments.

constraints.title: Compaction Memory Flush Transcript Size Threshold
constraints.description: Forces pre-compaction memory flush when transcript file size reaches this threshold (bytes or strings like "2mb"). Use this to prevent long-session hangs even when token counters are stale; set to 0 to disable.

constraints.title: Compaction Memory Flush Prompt
constraints.description: User-prompt template used for the pre-compaction memory flush turn when generating memory candidates. Use this only when you need custom extraction instructions beyond the default memory flush behavior.

constraints.title: Compaction Memory Flush Soft Threshold
constraints.description: Threshold distance to compaction (in tokens) that triggers pre-compaction memory flush execution. Use earlier thresholds for safer persistence, or tighter thresholds for lower flush frequency.

constraints.title: Compaction Memory Flush System Prompt
constraints.description: System-prompt override for the pre-compaction memory flush turn to control extraction style and safety constraints. Use carefully so custom instructions do not reduce memory quality or leak sensitive context.

constraints.title: Compaction Mode
constraints.description: Compaction strategy mode: "default" uses baseline behavior, while "safeguard" applies stricter guardrails to preserve recent context. Keep "default" unless you observe aggressive history loss near limit boundaries.

constraints.title: Compaction Model Override
constraints.description: Optional provider/model override used only for compaction summarization. Set this when you want compaction to run on a different model than the session default, and leave it unset to keep using the primary agent model.

constraints.title: Compaction Notify User
constraints.description: When enabled, sends a brief compaction notice to the user (e.g. '🧹 Compacting context...') when compaction starts. Disabled by default to keep compaction silent and non-intrusive.

constraints.title: Post-Compaction Context Sections
constraints.description: AGENTS.md H2/H3 section names re-injected after compaction so the agent reruns critical startup guidance. Leave unset to use "Session Startup"/"Red Lines" with legacy fallback to "Every Session"/"Safety"; set to [] to disable reinjection entirely.

constraints.title: Compaction Post-Index Sync
constraints.description: Controls post-compaction session memory reindex mode: "off", "async", or "await" (default: "async"). Use "await" for strongest freshness, "async" for lower compaction latency, and "off" only when session-memory sync is handled elsewhere.

constraints.title: Compaction Provider
constraints.description: Id of a registered compaction provider plugin used for summarization. When set and the provider is registered, its summarize() method is called instead of the built-in summarizeInStages pipeline. Falls back to built-in on provider failure. Leave unset to use the default built-in summarization.

constraints.title: Compaction Quality Guard
constraints.description: Optional quality-audit retry settings for safeguard compaction summaries. Leave this disabled unless you explicitly want summary audits and one-shot regeneration on failed checks.

constraints.title: Compaction Quality Guard Enabled
constraints.description: Enables summary quality audits and regeneration retries for safeguard compaction. Default: false, so safeguard mode alone does not turn on retry behavior.

constraints.title: Compaction Quality Guard Max Retries
constraints.description: Maximum number of regeneration retries after a failed safeguard summary quality audit. Use small values to bound extra latency and token cost.

constraints.title: Compaction Preserve Recent Turns
constraints.description: Number of most recent user/assistant turns kept verbatim outside safeguard summarization (default: 3). Raise this to preserve exact recent dialogue context, or lower it to maximize compaction savings.

constraints.title: Compaction Reserve Tokens
constraints.description: Token headroom reserved for reply generation and tool output after compaction runs. Use higher reserves for verbose/tool-heavy sessions, and lower reserves when maximizing retained history matters more.

constraints.title: Compaction Reserve Token Floor
constraints.description: Minimum floor enforced for reserveTokens in Pi compaction paths (0 disables the floor guard). Use a non-zero floor to avoid over-aggressive compression under fluctuating token estimates.

constraints.title: Compaction Timeout (Seconds)
constraints.description: Maximum time in seconds allowed for a single compaction operation before it is aborted (default: 900). Increase this for very large sessions that need more time to summarize, or decrease it to fail faster on unresponsive models.

constraints.title: Context Injection
constraints.description: Controls when workspace bootstrap files are injected into the system prompt: "always" (default) or "continuation-skip" for safe continuation turns after a completed assistant response.

constraints.title: Default Context Limits
constraints.description: Focused per-agent-context budget defaults for selected high-volume excerpts and injected prompt blocks. Use this to tune bounded read/injection sizes without reopening any unbounded call paths.

constraints.title: Default memory_get Line Window
constraints.description: Default memory_get line window used when requests omit lines. This controls how many source lines are selected before the max-char cap is applied.

constraints.title: Default memory_get Max Chars
constraints.description: Default max characters returned by memory_get before truncation metadata and continuation notice are added. Increase to approximate older larger excerpts, but keep it bounded.

constraints.title: Default Post-compaction Max Chars
constraints.description: Default max characters retained from AGENTS.md during post-compaction context refresh injection. Lower this to make compaction recovery cheaper, or raise it for agents that depend on longer startup guidance.

constraints.title: Default Tool Result Max Chars
constraints.description: Default max characters kept for a single live tool result before truncation. This affects both persisted live tool-result writes and overflow-recovery truncation heuristics.

constraints.title: Default Embedded Harness
constraints.description: Default embedded agent harness policy. Use runtime=auto for plugin harness selection, runtime=pi for built-in PI, or a registered harness id such as codex.

constraints.title: Default Embedded Harness Fallback
constraints.description: Embedded harness fallback when no plugin harness matches or an auto-selected plugin harness fails before side effects. Set none to disable automatic PI fallback.

constraints.title: Default Embedded Harness Runtime
constraints.description: Embedded harness runtime: auto, pi, or a registered plugin harness id such as codex.

constraints.title: Embedded Pi
constraints.description: Embedded Pi runner hardening controls for how workspace-local Pi settings are trusted and applied in OpenClaw sessions.

constraints.title: Embedded Pi Execution Contract
constraints.description: Embedded Pi execution contract: "default" keeps the standard runner behavior, while "strict-agentic" keeps OpenAI/OpenAI Codex GPT-5-family runs acting until they hit a real blocker instead of stopping at plans or filler.

constraints.title: Embedded Pi Project Settings Policy
constraints.description: How embedded Pi handles workspace-local `.pi/config/settings.json`: "sanitize" (default) strips shellPath/shellCommandPrefix, "ignore" disables project settings entirely, and "trusted" applies project settings as-is.

constraints.title: Envelope Elapsed
constraints.description: Include elapsed time in message envelopes ("on" or "off").

constraints.title: Envelope Timestamp
constraints.description: Include absolute timestamps in message envelopes ("on" or "off").

constraints.title: Envelope Timezone
constraints.description: Timezone for message envelopes ("utc", "local", "user", or an IANA timezone string).

constraints.title: Experimental Agent Flags
constraints.description: Experimental agent-default flags. Keep these off unless you are intentionally testing a preview surface.

constraints.title: Enable Lean Local Model Mode (Experimental)
constraints.description: Experimental local-model prompt trim. When enabled, OpenClaw drops heavyweight default tools like browser, cron, and message for weaker or smaller local-model backends.

constraints.title: Heartbeat Direct Policy
constraints.description: Controls whether heartbeat delivery may target direct/DM chats: "allow" (default) permits DM delivery and "block" suppresses direct-target sends.

constraints.title: Heartbeat Include System Prompt Section
constraints.description: Includes the default agent's ## Heartbeats system prompt section when true. Turn this off to keep heartbeat runtime behavior while omitting the heartbeat prompt instructions from the agent system prompt.

constraints.title: Heartbeat Suppress Tool Error Warnings
constraints.description: Suppress tool error warning payloads during heartbeat runs.

constraints.title: Heartbeat Timeout (Seconds)
constraints.description: Maximum time in seconds allowed for a heartbeat agent turn before it is aborted. Leave unset to use agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds.

constraints.title: Human Delay Max (ms)
constraints.description: Maximum delay in ms for custom humanDelay (default: 2500).

constraints.title: Human Delay Min (ms)
constraints.description: Minimum delay in ms for custom humanDelay (default: 800).

constraints.title: Human Delay Mode
constraints.description: Delay style for block replies ("off", "natural", "custom").

primary.title: Image Generation Model
primary.description: Optional image-generation model (provider/model) used by the shared image generation capability.

constraints.title: Image Max Dimension (px)
constraints.description: Max image side length in pixels when sanitizing transcript/tool-result image payloads (default: 1200).

primary.title: Image Model
primary.description: Optional image model (provider/model) used when the primary model lacks image input.

constraints.description: Idle timeout for LLM streaming responses in seconds. If no token is received within this time, the request is aborted. Set to 0 to disable. Default: 120 seconds.

constraints.title: Media Generation Auto Provider Fallback
constraints.description: When true (default), shared image, music, and video generation automatically appends other auth-backed provider defaults after explicit primary/fallback refs. Set false to disable implicit cross-provider fallback while keeping explicit fallbacks.

constraints.title: Memory Search
constraints.description: Vector search over MEMORY.md and memory/*.md (per-agent overrides supported).

constraints.title: Memory Search Embedding Cache
constraints.description: Caches computed chunk embeddings in SQLite so reindexing and incremental updates run faster (default: true). Keep this enabled unless investigating cache correctness or minimizing disk usage.

constraints.title: Memory Search Embedding Cache Max Entries
constraints.description: Sets a best-effort upper bound on cached embeddings kept in SQLite for memory search. Use this when controlling disk growth matters more than peak reindex speed.

constraints.title: Memory Chunk Overlap Tokens
constraints.description: Token overlap between adjacent memory chunks to preserve context continuity near split boundaries. Use modest overlap to reduce boundary misses without inflating index size too aggressively.

constraints.title: Memory Chunk Tokens
constraints.description: Chunk size in tokens used when splitting memory sources before embedding/indexing. Increase for broader context per chunk, or lower to improve precision on pinpoint lookups.

constraints.title: Enable Memory Search
constraints.description: Master toggle for memory search indexing and retrieval behavior on this agent profile. Keep enabled for semantic recall, and disable when you want fully stateless responses.

constraints.title: Memory Search Session Index (Experimental)
constraints.description: Indexes session transcripts into memory search so responses can reference prior chat turns. Keep this off unless transcript recall is needed, because indexing cost and storage usage both increase.

constraints.title: Extra Memory Paths
constraints.description: Adds extra directories or .md files to the memory index beyond default memory files. Use this when key reference docs live elsewhere in your repo; when multimodal memory is enabled, matching image/audio files under these paths are also eligible for indexing.

constraints.title: Memory Search Fallback
constraints.description: Backup provider used when primary embeddings fail: "openai", "gemini", "voyage", "mistral", "bedrock", "lmstudio", "ollama", "local", or "none". Set a real fallback for production reliability; use "none" only if you prefer explicit failures.

constraints.title: Local Embedding Model Path
constraints.description: Specifies the local embedding model source for local memory search, such as a GGUF file path or `hf:` URI. Use this only when provider is `local`, and verify model compatibility before large index rebuilds.

constraints.title: Memory Search Model
constraints.description: Embedding model override used by the selected memory provider when a non-default model is required. Set this only when you need explicit recall quality/cost tuning beyond provider defaults.

constraints.title: Memory Search Multimodal
constraints.description: Optional multimodal memory settings for indexing image and audio files from configured extra paths. Keep this off unless your embedding model explicitly supports cross-modal embeddings, and set `memorySearch.fallback` to "none" while it is enabled. Matching files are uploaded to the configured remote embedding provider during indexing.

constraints.title: Enable Memory Search Multimodal
constraints.description: Enables image/audio memory indexing from extraPaths. This currently requires Gemini embedding-2, keeps the default memory roots Markdown-only, disables memory-search fallback providers, and uploads matching binary content to the configured remote embedding provider.

constraints.title: Memory Search Multimodal Max File Bytes
constraints.description: Sets the maximum bytes allowed per multimodal file before it is skipped during memory indexing. Use this to cap upload cost and indexing latency, or raise it for short high-quality audio clips.

constraints.title: Memory Search Multimodal Modalities
constraints.description: Selects which multimodal file types are indexed from extraPaths: "image", "audio", or "all". Keep this narrow to avoid indexing large binary corpora unintentionally.

constraints.title: Memory Search Output Dimensionality
constraints.description: Provider-specific output vector size override for memory embeddings. Gemini embedding-2 supports 768, 1536, or 3072; Bedrock families such as Titan V2, Cohere V4, and Nova expose their own allowed sizes. Expect a full reindex when you change it because stored vector dimensions must stay consistent.

constraints.title: Memory Search Provider
constraints.description: Selects the embedding backend used to build/query memory vectors: "openai", "gemini", "voyage", "mistral", "bedrock", "lmstudio", "ollama", or "local". Keep your most reliable provider here and configure fallback for resilience.

constraints.title: Memory Search QMD Collections
constraints.description: Use this when one agent should query another agent's transcript collections; QMD-specific extra collections let you opt into cross-agent memory search without flattening everything into one shared namespace.

constraints.title: QMD Extra Collections
constraints.description: Use this when you need directional transcript search across agents; add collections here to scope QMD recalls without creating a shared global transcript namespace.

constraints.title: Memory Search Hybrid Candidate Multiplier
constraints.description: Expands the candidate pool before reranking (default: 4). Raise this for better recall on noisy corpora, but expect more compute and slightly slower searches.

constraints.title: Memory Search Hybrid
constraints.description: Combines BM25 keyword matching with vector similarity for better recall on mixed exact + semantic queries. Keep enabled unless you are isolating ranking behavior for troubleshooting.

constraints.title: Memory Search MMR Re-ranking
constraints.description: Adds MMR reranking to diversify results and reduce near-duplicate snippets in a single answer window. Enable when recall looks repetitive; keep off for strict score ordering.

constraints.title: Memory Search MMR Lambda
constraints.description: Sets MMR relevance-vs-diversity balance (0 = most diverse, 1 = most relevant, default: 0.7). Lower values reduce repetition; higher values keep tightly relevant but may duplicate.

constraints.title: Memory Search Temporal Decay
constraints.description: Applies recency decay so newer memory can outrank older memory when scores are close. Enable when timeliness matters; keep off for timeless reference knowledge.

constraints.title: Memory Search Temporal Decay Half-life (Days)
constraints.description: Controls how fast older memory loses rank when temporal decay is enabled (half-life in days, default: 30). Lower values prioritize recent context more aggressively.

constraints.title: Memory Search Text Weight
constraints.description: Controls how strongly BM25 keyword relevance influences hybrid ranking (0-1). Increase for exact-term matching; decrease when semantic matches should rank higher.

constraints.title: Memory Search Vector Weight
constraints.description: Controls how strongly semantic similarity influences hybrid ranking (0-1). Increase when paraphrase matching matters more than exact terms; decrease for stricter keyword emphasis.

constraints.title: Memory Search Max Results
constraints.description: Maximum number of memory hits returned from search before downstream reranking and prompt injection. Raise for broader recall, or lower for tighter prompts and faster responses.

constraints.title: Memory Search Min Score
constraints.description: Minimum relevance score threshold for including memory results in final recall output. Increase to reduce weak/noisy matches, or lower when you need more permissive retrieval.

constraints.title: Remote Embedding API Key
constraints.description: Supplies a dedicated API key for remote embedding calls used by memory indexing and query-time embeddings. Use this when memory embeddings should use different credentials than global defaults or environment variables.

constraints.title: Remote Embedding Base URL
constraints.description: Overrides the embedding API endpoint, such as an OpenAI-compatible proxy or custom Gemini base URL. Use this only when routing through your own gateway or vendor endpoint; keep provider defaults otherwise.

constraints.title: Remote Batch Concurrency
constraints.description: Limits how many embedding batch jobs run at the same time during indexing (default: 2). Increase carefully for faster bulk indexing, but watch provider rate limits and queue errors.

constraints.title: Remote Batch Embedding Enabled
constraints.description: Enables provider batch APIs for embedding jobs when supported (OpenAI/Gemini), improving throughput on larger index runs. Keep this enabled unless debugging provider batch failures or running very small workloads.

constraints.title: Remote Batch Poll Interval (ms)
constraints.description: Controls how often the system polls provider APIs for batch job status in milliseconds (default: 2000). Use longer intervals to reduce API chatter, or shorter intervals for faster completion detection.

constraints.title: Remote Batch Timeout (min)
constraints.description: Sets the maximum wait time for a full embedding batch operation in minutes (default: 60). Increase for very large corpora or slower providers, and lower it to fail fast in automation-heavy flows.

constraints.title: Remote Batch Wait for Completion
constraints.description: Waits for batch embedding jobs to fully finish before the indexing operation completes. Keep this enabled for deterministic indexing state; disable only if you accept delayed consistency.

constraints.title: Remote Embedding Headers
constraints.description: Adds custom HTTP headers to remote embedding requests, merged with provider defaults. Use this for proxy auth and tenant routing headers, and keep values minimal to avoid leaking sensitive metadata.

constraints.title: Memory Search Sources
constraints.description: Chooses which sources are indexed: "memory" reads MEMORY.md + memory files, and "sessions" includes transcript history. Keep ["memory"] unless you need recall from prior chat transcripts.

constraints.title: Memory Search Index Path
constraints.description: Sets where the SQLite memory index is stored on disk for each agent. Keep the default `~/.openclaw/memory/{agentId}.sqlite` unless you need custom storage placement or backup policy alignment.

constraints.title: Memory Search Vector Index
constraints.description: Enables the sqlite-vec extension used for vector similarity queries in memory search (default: true). Keep this enabled for normal semantic recall; disable only for debugging or fallback-only operation.

constraints.title: Memory Search Vector Extension Path
constraints.description: Overrides the auto-discovered sqlite-vec extension library path (`.dylib`, `.so`, or `.dll`). Use this when your runtime cannot find sqlite-vec automatically or you pin a known-good build.

constraints.title: Index on Search (Lazy)
constraints.description: Uses lazy sync by scheduling reindex on search after content changes are detected. Keep enabled for lower idle overhead, or disable if you require pre-synced indexes before any query.

constraints.title: Index on Session Start
constraints.description: Triggers a memory index sync when a session starts so early turns see fresh memory content. Keep enabled when startup freshness matters more than initial turn latency.

constraints.title: Session Delta Bytes
constraints.description: Requires at least this many newly appended bytes before session transcript changes trigger reindex (default: 100000). Increase to reduce frequent small reindexes, or lower for faster transcript freshness.

constraints.title: Session Delta Messages
constraints.description: Requires at least this many appended transcript messages before reindex is triggered (default: 50). Lower this for near-real-time transcript recall, or raise it to reduce indexing churn.

constraints.title: Force Reindex After Compaction
constraints.description: Forces a session memory-search reindex after compaction-triggered transcript updates (default: true). Keep enabled when compacted summaries must be immediately searchable, or disable to reduce write-time indexing pressure.

constraints.title: Watch Memory Files
constraints.description: Watches memory files and schedules index updates from file-change events (chokidar). Enable for near-real-time freshness; disable on very large workspaces if watch churn is too noisy.

constraints.title: Memory Watch Debounce (ms)
constraints.description: Debounce window in milliseconds for coalescing rapid file-watch events before reindex runs. Increase to reduce churn on frequently-written files, or lower for faster freshness.

fallbacks.title: Model Fallbacks
fallbacks.description: Ordered fallback models (provider/model). Used when the primary model fails.

constraints.title: Models
constraints.description: Configured model catalog (keys are full provider/model IDs).

primary.title: Music Generation Model
primary.description: Optional music-generation model (provider/model) used by the shared music generation capability.

constraints.title: PDF Max Size (MB)
constraints.description: Maximum PDF file size in megabytes for the PDF tool (default: 10).

constraints.title: PDF Max Pages
constraints.description: Maximum number of PDF pages to process for the PDF tool (default: 20).

primary.title: PDF Model
primary.description: Optional PDF model (provider/model) for the PDF analysis tool. Defaults to imageModel, then session model.

constraints.title: Repo Root
constraints.description: Optional repository root shown in the system prompt runtime line (overrides auto-detect).

constraints.title: Sandbox Browser CDP Source Port Range
constraints.description: Optional CIDR allowlist for container-edge CDP ingress (for example 172.21.0.1/32).

constraints.title: Sandbox Browser Network
constraints.description: Docker network for sandbox browser containers (default: openclaw-sandbox-browser). Avoid bridge if you need stricter isolation.

constraints.title: Sandbox Docker Allow Container Namespace Join
constraints.description: DANGEROUS break-glass override that allows sandbox Docker network mode container:<id>. This joins another container namespace and weakens sandbox isolation.

constraints.title: Skills
constraints.description: Optional default skill allowlist inherited by agents that omit agents.list[].skills. Omit for unrestricted skills, set [] to give inheriting agents no skills, and remember explicit agents.list[].skills replaces this default instead of merging with it.

constraints.title: Startup Context
constraints.description: Runtime-owned first-turn prelude for bare "/new" and "/reset". Use this to control whether recent daily memory files are preloaded into the first prompt instead of asking the model to decide what to read.

constraints.title: Startup Context Apply On
constraints.description: Chooses which bare reset commands get startup context: include "new", "reset", or both (default: ["new","reset"]).

constraints.title: Startup Context Daily Memory Days
constraints.description: Number of dated memory files to load counting backward from today in the configured user timezone (default: 2 for today + yesterday).

constraints.title: Enable Startup Context
constraints.description: Enable the startup-context prelude for bare session resets (default: true). Disable this to fall back to prompt-only behavior with no runtime-loaded daily memory.

constraints.title: Startup Context Max File Bytes
constraints.description: Maximum bytes allowed per daily memory file when building startup context (default: 16384). Files over this boundary-safe read limit are skipped.

constraints.title: Startup Context Max File Chars
constraints.description: Maximum characters retained from each loaded daily memory file in the startup prelude (default: 1200).

constraints.title: Startup Context Max Total Chars
constraints.description: Maximum total characters retained across all loaded daily memory files in the startup prelude (default: 2800). Additional files are truncated from the prelude once this cap is reached.
Maximum number of active children a single agent session can spawn (default: 5).
Maximum nesting depth for sub-agent spawning. 1 = no nesting (default), 2 = sub-agents can spawn sub-sub-agents.

primary.title: Video Generation Model
primary.description: Optional video-generation model (provider/model) used by the shared video generation capability.

constraints.title: Workspace
constraints.description: Default workspace path exposed to agent runtime tools for filesystem context and repo-aware behavior. Set this explicitly when running from wrappers so path resolution stays deterministic.

runtime.title: Agent Embedded Harness Runtime
runtime.description: Per-agent embedded harness runtime: auto, pi, or a registered plugin harness id such as codex.

fallback.title: Agent Embedded Harness Fallback
fallback.description: Per-agent embedded harness fallback. Set none to disable automatic PI fallback for this agent.

embeddedHarness.title: Agent Embedded Harness
embeddedHarness.description: Per-agent embedded harness policy override. Use fallback=none to make this agent fail instead of falling back to PI.

thinkingDefault.title: Agent Thinking Default
thinkingDefault.description: Optional per-agent default thinking level. Overrides agents.defaults.thinkingDefault for this agent when no per-message or session override is set.

reasoningDefault.title: Agent Reasoning Default
reasoningDefault.description: Optional per-agent default reasoning visibility (on|off|stream). Applies when no per-message or session reasoning override is set.

fastModeDefault.title: Agent Fast Mode Default
fastModeDefault.description: Optional per-agent default for fast mode. Applies when no per-message or session fast-mode override is set.

skills.title: Agent Skill Filter
skills.description: Optional allowlist of skills for this agent. If omitted, the agent inherits agents.defaults.skills when set; otherwise skills stay unrestricted. Set [] for no skills. An explicit list fully replaces inherited defaults instead of merging with them.

maxSkillsPromptChars.title: Agent Skills Prompt Max Chars
maxSkillsPromptChars.description: Per-agent override for the skills prompt character budget. This extends the existing skills.limits.maxSkillsPromptChars path instead of routing the same budget through contextLimits.

skillsLimits.title: Agent Skills Limits
skillsLimits.description: Optional per-agent overrides for skills subsystem budgets. Use this when an agent needs a different skills prompt budget without introducing a second generic context-limits path.

memoryGetMaxChars.title: Agent memory_get Max Chars
memoryGetMaxChars.description: Per-agent override for the default memory_get max character budget.

memoryGetDefaultLines.title: Agent memory_get Line Window
memoryGetDefaultLines.description: Per-agent override for the default memory_get line window when lines is omitted.

toolResultMaxChars.title: Agent Tool Result Max Chars
toolResultMaxChars.description: Per-agent override for the live tool-result max character budget.

postCompactionMaxChars.title: Agent Post-compaction Max Chars
postCompactionMaxChars.description: Per-agent override for the post-compaction AGENTS.md excerpt budget.

contextLimits.title: Agent Context Limits
contextLimits.description: Optional per-agent overrides for the focused context budget knobs. Omitted fields inherit agents.defaults.contextLimits.

directPolicy.title: Heartbeat Direct Policy
directPolicy.description: Per-agent override for heartbeat direct/DM delivery policy; use "block" for agents that should only send heartbeat alerts to non-DM destinations.

includeSystemPromptSection.title: Heartbeat Include System Prompt Section
includeSystemPromptSection.description: Per-agent override for whether the default agent's ## Heartbeats system prompt section is injected. Use false to keep heartbeat runtime behavior but omit the heartbeat prompt instructions from that agent's system prompt.

suppressToolErrorWarnings.title: Heartbeat Suppress Tool Error Warnings
suppressToolErrorWarnings.description: Suppress tool error warning payloads during heartbeat runs.

timeoutSeconds.title: Heartbeat Timeout (Seconds)
timeoutSeconds.description: Per-agent maximum time in seconds allowed for a heartbeat agent turn before it is aborted. Leave unset to inherit the merged heartbeat/default agent timeout.

avatar.title: Identity Avatar
avatar.description: Agent avatar (workspace-relative path, http(s) URL, or data URI).

executionContract.title: Agent Embedded Pi Execution Contract
executionContract.description: Optional per-agent embedded Pi execution contract override. Set "strict-agentic" to keep that agent acting through plan-only turns on OpenAI/OpenAI Codex GPT-5-family runs, or "default" to inherit the standard runner behavior.

embeddedPi.title: Agent Embedded Pi
embeddedPi.description: Optional per-agent embedded Pi overrides. Use this to opt specific agents into stricter GPT-5 execution behavior without changing the global default.

dangerouslyAllowContainerNamespaceJoin.title: Agent Sandbox Docker Allow Container Namespace Join
dangerouslyAllowContainerNamespaceJoin.description: Per-agent DANGEROUS override for container namespace joins in sandbox Docker network mode.

network.title: Agent Sandbox Browser Network
network.description: Per-agent override for sandbox browser Docker network.

cdpSourceRange.title: Agent Sandbox Browser CDP Source Port Range
cdpSourceRange.description: Per-agent override for CDP source CIDR allowlist.

profile.title: Agent Tool Profile
profile.description: Per-agent override for tool profile selection when one agent needs a different capability baseline. Use this sparingly so policy differences across agents stay intentional and reviewable.

alsoAllow.title: Agent Tool Allowlist Additions
alsoAllow.description: Per-agent additive allowlist for tools on top of global and profile policy. Keep narrow to avoid accidental privilege expansion on specialized agents.

byProvider.title: Agent Tool Policy by Provider
byProvider.description: Per-agent provider-specific tool policy overrides for channel-scoped capability control. Use this when a single agent needs tighter restrictions on one provider than others.

type.title: Agent Runtime Type
type.description: Runtime type for this agent: "embedded" (default OpenClaw runtime) or "acp" (ACP harness defaults).

type.title: Agent Runtime Type
type.description: Runtime type for this agent: "embedded" (default OpenClaw runtime) or "acp" (ACP harness defaults).

agent.title: Agent ACP Harness Agent
agent.description: Optional ACP harness agent id to use for this OpenClaw agent (for example codex, claude, cursor, gemini, openclaw).

backend.title: Agent ACP Backend
backend.description: Optional ACP backend override for this agent's ACP sessions (falls back to global acp.backend).

mode.title: Agent ACP Mode
mode.description: Optional ACP session mode default for this agent (persistent or oneshot).

cwd.title: Agent ACP Working Directory
cwd.description: Optional default working directory for this agent's ACP sessions.

acp.title: Agent ACP Runtime
acp.description: ACP runtime defaults for this agent when runtime.type=acp. Binding-level ACP overrides still take precedence per conversation.

runtime.title: Agent Runtime
runtime.description: Optional runtime descriptor for this agent. Use embedded for default OpenClaw execution or acp for external ACP harness defaults.

constraints.title: Agent List
constraints.description: Explicit list of configured agents with IDs and optional overrides for model, tools, identity, and workspace. Keep IDs stable over time so bindings, approvals, and session routing remain deterministic.

constraints.title: Approvals
constraints.description: Approval routing controls for forwarding exec and plugin approval requests to chat destinations outside the originating session. Keep these disabled unless operators need explicit out-of-band approval visibility.

constraints.title: Exec Approval Forwarding
constraints.description: Groups exec-approval forwarding behavior including enablement, routing mode, filters, and explicit targets. Configure here when approval prompts must reach operational channels instead of only the origin thread.

constraints.title: Approval Agent Filter
constraints.description: Optional allowlist of agent IDs eligible for forwarded approvals, for example `["primary", "ops-agent"]`. Use this to limit forwarding blast radius and avoid notifying channels for unrelated agents.

constraints.title: Forward Exec Approvals
constraints.description: Enables forwarding of exec approval requests to configured delivery destinations (default: false). Keep disabled in low-risk setups and enable only when human approval responders need channel-visible prompts.

constraints.title: Approval Forwarding Mode
constraints.description: Controls where approval prompts are sent: "session" uses origin chat, "targets" uses configured targets, and "both" sends to both paths. Use "session" as baseline and expand only when operational workflow requires redundancy.

constraints.title: Approval Session Filter
constraints.description: Optional session-key filters matched as substring or regex-style patterns, for example `["discord:", "^agent:ops:"]`. Use narrow patterns so only intended approval contexts are forwarded to shared destinations.

channel.title: Approval Target Channel
channel.description: Channel/provider ID used for forwarded approval delivery, such as discord, slack, or a plugin channel id. Use valid channel IDs only so approvals do not silently fail due to unknown routes.

to.title: Approval Target Destination
to.description: Destination identifier inside the target channel (channel ID, user ID, or thread root depending on provider). Verify semantics per provider because destination format differs across channel integrations.

accountId.title: Approval Target Account ID
accountId.description: Optional account selector for multi-account channel setups when approvals must route through a specific account context. Use this only when the target channel has multiple configured identities.

threadId.title: Approval Target Thread ID
threadId.description: Optional thread/topic target for channels that support threaded delivery of forwarded approvals. Use this to keep approval traffic contained in operational threads instead of main channels.

constraints.title: Approval Forwarding Targets
constraints.description: Explicit delivery targets used when forwarding mode includes targets, each with channel and destination details. Keep target lists least-privilege and validate each destination before enabling broad forwarding.

constraints.title: Plugin Approval Forwarding
constraints.description: Groups plugin-approval forwarding behavior including enablement, routing mode, filters, and explicit targets. Independent of exec approval forwarding. Configure here when plugin approval prompts must reach operational channels.

constraints.title: Plugin Approval Agent Filter
constraints.description: Optional allowlist of agent IDs eligible for forwarded plugin approvals, for example `["primary", "ops-agent"]`. Use this to limit forwarding blast radius.

constraints.title: Forward Plugin Approvals
constraints.description: Enables forwarding of plugin approval requests to configured delivery destinations (default: false). Independent of approvals.exec.enabled.

constraints.title: Plugin Approval Forwarding Mode
constraints.description: Controls where plugin approval prompts are sent: "session" uses origin chat, "targets" uses configured targets, and "both" sends to both paths.

constraints.title: Plugin Approval Session Filter
constraints.description: Optional session-key filters matched as substring or regex-style patterns, for example `["discord:", "^agent:ops:"]`. Use narrow patterns so only intended approval contexts are forwarded.

channel.title: Plugin Approval Target Channel
channel.description: Channel/provider ID used for forwarded plugin approval delivery, such as discord, slack, or a plugin channel id.

to.title: Plugin Approval Target Destination
to.description: Destination identifier inside the target channel (channel ID, user ID, or thread root depending on provider).

accountId.title: Plugin Approval Target Account ID
accountId.description: Optional account selector for multi-account channel setups when plugin approvals must route through a specific account context.

threadId.title: Plugin Approval Target Thread ID
threadId.description: Optional thread/topic target for channels that support threaded delivery of forwarded plugin approvals.

constraints.title: Plugin Approval Forwarding Targets
constraints.description: Explicit delivery targets used when plugin approval forwarding mode includes targets, each with channel and destination details.

constraints.title: Audio
constraints.description: Global audio ingestion settings used before higher-level tools process speech or media content. Configure this when you need deterministic transcription behavior for voice notes and clips.

constraints.title: Audio Transcription
constraints.description: Command-based transcription settings for converting audio files into text before agent handling. Keep a simple, deterministic command path here so failures are easy to diagnose in logs.

constraints.title: Audio Transcription Command
constraints.description: Executable + args used to transcribe audio (first token must be a safe binary/path), for example `["whisper-cli", "--model", "small", "{input}"]`. Prefer a pinned command so runtime environments behave consistently.

constraints.title: Audio Transcription Timeout (sec)
constraints.description: Maximum time allowed for the transcription command to finish before it is aborted. Increase this for longer recordings, and keep it tight in latency-sensitive deployments.

constraints.title: Auth
constraints.description: Authentication profile root used for multi-profile provider credentials and cooldown-based failover ordering. Keep profiles minimal and explicit so automatic failover behavior stays auditable.

constraints.title: Auth Cooldowns
constraints.description: Cooldown/backoff controls for temporary profile suppression after billing-related failures and retry windows. Use these to prevent rapid re-selection of profiles that are still blocked.

constraints.title: Auth-Permanent Backoff (minutes)
constraints.description: Base backoff (minutes) for high-confidence auth_permanent failures (default: 10). Keep this shorter than billing so providers recover automatically after transient upstream auth incidents.

constraints.title: Auth-Permanent Backoff Cap (minutes)
constraints.description: Cap (minutes) for auth_permanent backoff (default: 60).

constraints.title: Billing Backoff (hours)
constraints.description: Base backoff (hours) when a profile fails due to billing/insufficient credits (default: 5).

constraints.title: Billing Backoff Overrides
constraints.description: Optional per-provider overrides for billing backoff (hours).

constraints.title: Billing Backoff Cap (hours)
constraints.description: Cap (hours) for billing backoff (default: 24).

constraints.title: Failover Window (hours)
constraints.description: Failure window (hours) for backoff counters (default: 24).

constraints.title: Overloaded Backoff (ms)
constraints.description: Fixed delay in milliseconds before retrying an overloaded provider/profile rotation (default: 0).

constraints.title: Overloaded Profile Rotations
constraints.description: Maximum same-provider auth-profile rotations allowed for overloaded errors before switching to model fallback (default: 1).

constraints.title: Rate-Limited Profile Rotations
constraints.description: Maximum same-provider auth-profile rotations allowed for rate-limit errors before switching to model fallback (default: 1).

constraints.title: Auth Profile Order
constraints.description: Ordered auth profile IDs per provider (used for automatic failover).

constraints.title: Auth Profiles
constraints.description: Named auth profiles (provider + mode + optional email).

type.title: Binding Type
type.description: Binding kind. Use "route" (or omit for legacy route entries) for normal routing, and "acp" for persistent ACP conversation bindings.

agentId.title: Binding Agent ID
agentId.description: Target agent ID that receives traffic when the corresponding binding match rule is satisfied. Use valid configured agent IDs only so routing does not fail at runtime.

channel.title: Binding Channel
channel.description: Channel/provider identifier this binding applies to, such as `telegram`, `discord`, or a plugin channel ID. Use the configured channel key exactly so binding evaluation works reliably.

accountId.title: Binding Account ID
accountId.description: Optional account selector for multi-account channel setups so the binding applies only to one identity. Use this when account scoping is required for the route and leave unset otherwise.

kind.title: Binding Peer Kind
kind.description: Peer conversation type: "direct", "group", "channel", or legacy "dm" (deprecated alias for direct). Prefer "direct" for new configs and keep kind aligned with channel semantics.

id.title: Binding Peer ID
id.description: Conversation identifier used with peer matching, such as a chat ID, channel ID, or group ID from the provider. Keep this exact to avoid silent non-matches.

peer.title: Binding Peer Match
peer.description: Optional peer matcher for specific conversations including peer kind and peer id. Use this when only one direct/group/channel target should be pinned to an agent.

guildId.title: Binding Guild ID
guildId.description: Optional Discord-style guild/server ID constraint for binding evaluation in multi-server deployments. Use this when the same peer identifiers can appear across different guilds.

teamId.title: Binding Team ID
teamId.description: Optional team/workspace ID constraint used by providers that scope chats under teams. Add this when you need bindings isolated to one workspace context.

roles.title: Binding Roles
roles.description: Optional role-based filter list used by providers that attach roles to chat context. Use this to route privileged or operational role traffic to specialized agents.

match.title: Binding Match Rule
match.description: Match rule object for deciding when a binding applies, including channel and optional account/peer constraints. Keep rules narrow to avoid accidental agent takeover across contexts.

type.title: Binding Type
type.description: Binding kind. Use "route" (or omit for legacy route entries) for normal routing, and "acp" for persistent ACP conversation bindings.

agentId.title: Binding Agent ID
agentId.description: Target agent ID that receives traffic when the corresponding binding match rule is satisfied. Use valid configured agent IDs only so routing does not fail at runtime.

channel.title: Binding Channel
channel.description: Channel/provider identifier this binding applies to, such as `telegram`, `discord`, or a plugin channel ID. Use the configured channel key exactly so binding evaluation works reliably.

accountId.title: Binding Account ID
accountId.description: Optional account selector for multi-account channel setups so the binding applies only to one identity. Use this when account scoping is required for the route and leave unset otherwise.

kind.title: Binding Peer Kind
kind.description: Peer conversation type: "direct", "group", "channel", or legacy "dm" (deprecated alias for direct). Prefer "direct" for new configs and keep kind aligned with channel semantics.

id.title: Binding Peer ID
id.description: Conversation identifier used with peer matching, such as a chat ID, channel ID, or group ID from the provider. Keep this exact to avoid silent non-matches.

peer.title: Binding Peer Match
peer.description: Optional peer matcher for specific conversations including peer kind and peer id. Use this when only one direct/group/channel target should be pinned to an agent.

guildId.title: Binding Guild ID
guildId.description: Optional Discord-style guild/server ID constraint for binding evaluation in multi-server deployments. Use this when the same peer identifiers can appear across different guilds.

teamId.title: Binding Team ID
teamId.description: Optional team/workspace ID constraint used by providers that scope chats under teams. Add this when you need bindings isolated to one workspace context.

roles.title: Binding Roles
roles.description: Optional role-based filter list used by providers that attach roles to chat context. Use this to route privileged or operational role traffic to specialized agents.

match.title: Binding Match Rule
match.description: Match rule object for deciding when a binding applies, including channel and optional account/peer constraints. Keep rules narrow to avoid accidental agent takeover across contexts.

mode.title: ACP Binding Mode
mode.description: ACP session mode override for this binding (persistent or oneshot).

label.title: ACP Binding Label
label.description: Human-friendly label for ACP status/diagnostics in this bound conversation.

cwd.title: ACP Binding Working Directory
cwd.description: Working directory override for ACP sessions created from this binding.

backend.title: ACP Binding Backend
backend.description: ACP backend override for this binding (falls back to agent runtime ACP backend, then global acp.backend).

acp.title: ACP Binding Overrides
acp.description: Optional per-binding ACP overrides for bindings[].type=acp. This layer overrides agents.list[].runtime.acp defaults for the matched conversation.

constraints.title: Bindings
constraints.description: Top-level binding rules for routing and persistent ACP conversation ownership. Use type=route for normal routing and type=acp for persistent ACP harness bindings.

additionalProperties.title: Broadcast Destination List
additionalProperties.description: Per-source broadcast destination list where each key is a source peer ID and the value is an array of destination peer IDs. Keep lists intentional to avoid accidental message amplification.

constraints.title: Broadcast
constraints.description: Broadcast routing map for sending the same outbound message to multiple peer IDs per source conversation. Keep this minimal and audited because one source can fan out to many destinations.

constraints.title: Broadcast Strategy
constraints.description: Delivery order for broadcast fan-out: "parallel" sends to all targets concurrently, while "sequential" sends one-by-one. Use "parallel" for speed and "sequential" for stricter ordering/backpressure control.

constraints.title: Browser
constraints.description: Browser runtime controls for local or remote CDP attachment, profile routing, and screenshot/snapshot behavior. Keep defaults unless your automation workflow requires custom browser transport settings.

constraints.title: Browser Attach-only Mode
constraints.description: Restricts browser mode to attach-only behavior without starting local browser processes. Use this when all browser sessions are externally managed by a remote CDP provider.

constraints.title: Browser CDP Port Range Start
constraints.description: Starting local CDP port used for auto-allocated browser profile ports. Increase this when host-level port defaults conflict with other local services.

constraints.title: Browser CDP URL
constraints.description: Remote CDP websocket URL used to attach to an externally managed browser instance. Use this for centralized browser hosts and keep URL access restricted to trusted network paths.

constraints.title: Browser Accent Color
constraints.description: Default accent color used for browser profile/UI cues where colored identity hints are displayed. Use consistent colors to help operators identify active browser profile context quickly.

constraints.title: Browser Default Profile
constraints.description: Default browser profile name selected when callers do not explicitly choose a profile. Use a stable low-privilege profile as the default to reduce accidental cross-context state use.

constraints.title: Browser Enabled
constraints.description: Enables browser capability wiring in the gateway so browser tools and CDP-driven workflows can run. Disable when browser automation is not needed to reduce surface area and startup work.

constraints.title: Browser Evaluate Enabled
constraints.description: Enables browser-side evaluate helpers for runtime script evaluation capabilities where supported. Keep disabled unless your workflows require evaluate semantics beyond snapshots/navigation.

constraints.title: Browser Executable Path
constraints.description: Explicit browser executable path when auto-discovery is insufficient for your host environment. Use absolute stable paths so launch behavior stays deterministic across restarts.

constraints.title: Browser Headless Mode
constraints.description: Forces browser launch in headless mode when the local launcher starts browser instances. Keep headless enabled for server environments and disable only when visible UI debugging is required.

constraints.title: Browser No-Sandbox Mode
constraints.description: Disables Chromium sandbox isolation flags for environments where sandboxing fails at runtime. Keep this off whenever possible because process isolation protections are reduced.

cdpPort.title: Browser Profile CDP Port
cdpPort.description: Per-profile local CDP port used when connecting to browser instances by port instead of URL. Use unique ports per profile to avoid connection collisions.

cdpUrl.title: Browser Profile CDP URL
cdpUrl.description: Per-profile CDP websocket URL used for explicit remote browser routing by profile name. Use this when profile connections terminate on remote hosts or tunnels.

userDataDir.title: Browser Profile User Data Dir
userDataDir.description: Per-profile Chromium user data directory for existing-session attachment through Chrome DevTools MCP. Use this for host-local Brave, Edge, Chromium, or non-default Chrome profiles when the built-in auto-connect path would pick the wrong browser data directory.

driver.title: Browser Profile Driver
driver.description: Per-profile browser driver mode. Use "openclaw" (or legacy "clawd") for CDP-based profiles, or use "existing-session" for host-local Chrome DevTools MCP attachment.

attachOnly.title: Browser Profile Attach-only Mode
attachOnly.description: Per-profile attach-only override that skips local browser launch and only attaches to an existing CDP endpoint. Useful when one profile is externally managed but others are locally launched.

color.title: Browser Profile Accent Color
color.description: Per-profile accent color for visual differentiation in dashboards and browser-related UI hints. Use distinct colors for high-signal operator recognition of active profiles.

constraints.title: Browser Profiles
constraints.description: Named browser profile connection map used for explicit routing to CDP ports or URLs with optional metadata. Keep profile names consistent and avoid overlapping endpoint definitions.

constraints.title: Remote CDP Handshake Timeout (ms)
constraints.description: Timeout in milliseconds for post-connect CDP handshake readiness checks against remote browser targets. Raise this for slow-start remote browsers and lower to fail fast in automation loops.

constraints.title: Remote CDP Timeout (ms)
constraints.description: Timeout in milliseconds for connecting to a remote CDP endpoint before failing the browser attach attempt. Increase for high-latency tunnels, or lower for faster failure detection.

constraints.title: Browser Snapshot Defaults
constraints.description: Default snapshot capture configuration used when callers do not provide explicit snapshot options. Tune this for consistent capture behavior across channels and automation paths.

constraints.title: Browser Snapshot Mode
constraints.description: Default snapshot extraction mode controlling how page content is transformed for agent consumption. Choose the mode that balances readability, fidelity, and token footprint for your workflows.

constraints.title: Browser SSRF Policy
constraints.description: Server-side request forgery guardrail settings for browser/network fetch paths that could reach internal hosts. Keep restrictive defaults in production and open only explicitly approved targets.

constraints.title: Browser Allowed Hostnames
constraints.description: Explicit hostname allowlist exceptions for SSRF policy checks on browser/network requests. Keep this list minimal and review entries regularly to avoid stale broad access.

constraints.title: Browser Dangerously Allow Private Network
constraints.description: Allows access to private-network address ranges from browser tooling. Default is disabled when unset; enable only for explicitly trusted private-network destinations.

constraints.title: Browser Hostname Allowlist
constraints.description: Legacy/alternate hostname allowlist field used by SSRF policy consumers for explicit host exceptions. Use stable exact hostnames and avoid wildcard-like broad patterns.

constraints.title: Canvas Host
constraints.description: Canvas host settings for serving canvas assets and local live-reload behavior used by canvas-enabled workflows. Keep disabled unless canvas-hosted assets are actively used.

constraints.title: Canvas Host Enabled
constraints.description: Enables the canvas host server process and routes for serving canvas files. Keep disabled when canvas workflows are inactive to reduce exposed local services.

constraints.title: Canvas Host Live Reload
constraints.description: Enables automatic live-reload behavior for canvas assets during development workflows. Keep disabled in production-like environments where deterministic output is preferred.

constraints.title: Canvas Host Port
constraints.description: TCP port used by the canvas host HTTP server when canvas hosting is enabled. Choose a non-conflicting port and align firewall/proxy policy accordingly.

constraints.title: Canvas Host Root Directory
constraints.description: Filesystem root directory served by canvas host for canvas content and static assets. Use a dedicated directory and avoid broad repo roots for least-privilege file exposure.

constraints.title: Channels
constraints.description: Channel provider configurations plus shared defaults that control access policies, heartbeat visibility, and per-surface behavior. Keep defaults centralized and override per provider only where required.

dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork.description: Dangerous opt-in for trusted Telegram fake-IP or transparent-proxy environments where api.telegram.org resolves to private/internal/special-use addresses during media downloads.

webhookUrl.description: Public HTTPS webhook URL registered with Telegram for inbound updates. This must be internet-reachable and requires channels.telegram.webhookSecret.

webhookSecret.description: Secret token sent to Telegram during webhook registration and verified on inbound webhook requests. Telegram returns this value for verification; this is not the gateway auth token and not the bot token.

webhookPath.description: Local webhook route path served by the gateway listener. Defaults to /telegram-webhook.

webhookHost.description: Local bind host for the webhook listener. Defaults to 127.0.0.1; keep loopback unless you intentionally expose direct ingress.

webhookPort.description: Local bind port for the webhook listener. Defaults to 8787; set to 0 to let the OS assign an ephemeral port.

webhookCertPath.description: Path to the self-signed certificate (PEM) to upload to Telegram during webhook registration. Required for self-signed certs (direct IP or no domain).

trustedLocalFileRoots.description: Trusted local filesystem roots for self-hosted Telegram Bot API absolute file_path values. Only absolute paths under these roots are read directly; all other absolute paths are rejected.

constraints.description: Dangerous opt-in for trusted Telegram fake-IP or transparent-proxy environments where api.telegram.org resolves to private/internal/special-use addresses during media downloads.
Trusted local filesystem roots for self-hosted Telegram Bot API absolute file_path values. Only absolute paths under these roots are read directly; all other absolute paths are rejected.
Path to the self-signed certificate (PEM) to upload to Telegram during webhook registration. Required for self-signed certs (direct IP or no domain).
Local bind host for the webhook listener. Defaults to 127.0.0.1; keep loopback unless you intentionally expose direct ingress.
Local webhook route path served by the gateway listener. Defaults to /telegram-webhook.
Local bind port for the webhook listener. Defaults to 8787; set to 0 to let the OS assign an ephemeral port.

Secret token sent to Telegram during webhook registration and verified on inbound webhook requests. Telegram returns this value for verification; this is not the gateway auth token and not the bot token.
Public HTTPS webhook URL registered with Telegram for inbound updates. This must be internet-reachable and requires channels.telegram.webhookSecret.

constraints.title: CLI
constraints.description: CLI presentation controls for local command output behavior such as banner and tagline style. Use this section to keep startup output aligned with operator preference without changing runtime behavior.

constraints.title: CLI Banner
constraints.description: CLI startup banner controls for title/version line and tagline style behavior. Keep banner enabled for fast version/context checks, then tune tagline mode to your preferred noise level.

constraints.title: CLI Banner Tagline Mode
constraints.description: Controls tagline style in the CLI startup banner: "random" (default) picks from the rotating tagline pool, "default" always shows the neutral default tagline, and "off" hides tagline text while keeping the banner version line.

constraints.title: Commands
constraints.description: Controls chat command surfaces, owner gating, and elevated command access behavior across providers. Keep defaults unless you need stricter operator controls or broader command availability.

constraints.title: Command Elevated Access Rules
constraints.description: Defines elevated command allow rules by channel and sender for owner-level command surfaces. Use narrow provider-specific identities so privileged commands are not exposed to broad chat audiences.

constraints.title: Allow Bash Chat Command
constraints.description: Allow bash chat command (`!`; `/bash` alias) to run host shell commands (default: false; requires tools.elevated).

constraints.title: Bash Foreground Window (ms)
constraints.description: How long bash waits before backgrounding (default: 2000; 0 backgrounds immediately).

constraints.title: Allow /config
constraints.description: Allow /config chat command to read/write config on disk (default: false).

constraints.title: Allow /debug
constraints.description: Allow /debug chat command for runtime-only overrides (default: false).

constraints.title: Allow /mcp
constraints.description: Allow /mcp chat command to manage OpenClaw MCP server config under mcp.servers (default: false).

constraints.title: Native Commands
constraints.description: Registers native slash/menu commands with channels that support command registration (Discord, Slack, Telegram). Keep enabled for discoverability unless you intentionally run text-only command workflows.

constraints.title: Native Skill Commands
constraints.description: Registers native skill commands so users can invoke skills directly from provider command menus where supported. Keep aligned with your skill policy so exposed commands match what operators expect.

constraints.title: Command Owners
constraints.description: Explicit owner allowlist for owner-only tools/commands. Use channel-native IDs (optionally prefixed like "whatsapp:+15551234567"). '*' is ignored.

constraints.title: Owner ID Display
constraints.description: Controls how owner IDs are rendered in the system prompt. Allowed values: raw, hash. Default: raw.

constraints.title: Owner ID Hash Secret
constraints.description: Optional secret used to HMAC hash owner IDs when ownerDisplay=hash. Prefer env substitution.

constraints.title: Allow /plugins
constraints.description: Allow /plugins chat command to list discovered plugins and toggle plugin enablement in config (default: false).

constraints.title: Allow Restart
constraints.description: Allow /restart and gateway restart tool actions (default: true).

constraints.title: Text Commands
constraints.description: Enables text-command parsing in chat input in addition to native command surfaces where available. Keep this enabled for compatibility across channels that do not support native command registration.

constraints.title: Use Access Groups
constraints.description: Enforce access-group allowlists/policies for commands.

constraints.title: Cron
constraints.description: Global scheduler settings for stored cron jobs, run concurrency, delivery fallback, and run-session retention. Keep defaults unless you are scaling job volume or integrating external webhook receivers.

constraints.title: Cron Enabled
constraints.description: Enables cron job execution for stored schedules managed by the gateway. Keep enabled for normal reminder/automation flows, and disable only to pause all cron execution without deleting jobs.

constraints.title: Cron Max Concurrent Runs
constraints.description: Limits how many cron jobs can execute at the same time when multiple schedules fire together. Use lower values to protect CPU/memory under heavy automation load, or raise carefully for higher throughput.

constraints.title: Cron Retry Policy
constraints.description: Overrides the default retry policy for one-shot jobs when they fail with transient errors (rate limit, overloaded, network, server_error). Omit to use defaults: maxAttempts 3, backoffMs [30000, 60000, 300000], retry all transient types.

constraints.title: Cron Retry Backoff (ms)
constraints.description: Backoff delays in ms for each retry attempt (default: [30000, 60000, 300000]). Use shorter values for faster retries.

constraints.title: Cron Retry Max Attempts
constraints.description: Max retries for one-shot jobs on transient errors before permanent disable (default: 3).

constraints.title: Cron Retry Error Types
constraints.description: Error types to retry: rate_limit, overloaded, network, timeout, server_error. Use to restrict which errors trigger retries; omit to retry all transient types.

constraints.title: Cron Run Log Pruning
constraints.description: Pruning controls for per-job cron run history files under `cron/runs/<jobId>.jsonl`, including size and line retention.

constraints.title: Cron Run Log Keep Lines
constraints.description: How many trailing run-log lines to retain when a file exceeds maxBytes (default `2000`). Increase for longer forensic history or lower for smaller disks.

constraints.title: Cron Run Log Max Bytes
constraints.description: Maximum bytes per cron run-log file before pruning rewrites to the last keepLines entries (for example `2mb`, default `2000000`).

constraints.title: Cron Session Retention
constraints.description: Controls how long completed cron run sessions are kept before pruning (`24h`, `7d`, `1h30m`, or `false` to disable pruning; default: `24h`). Use shorter retention to reduce storage growth on high-frequency schedules.

constraints.title: Cron Store Path
constraints.description: Path to the cron job store file used to persist scheduled jobs across restarts. Set an explicit path only when you need custom storage layout, backups, or mounted volumes.

constraints.title: Cron Legacy Webhook (Deprecated)
constraints.description: Deprecated legacy fallback webhook URL used only for old jobs with `notify=true`. Migrate to per-job delivery using `delivery.mode="webhook"` plus `delivery.to`, and avoid relying on this global field.

constraints.title: Cron Webhook Bearer Token
constraints.description: Bearer token attached to cron webhook POST deliveries when webhook mode is used. Prefer secret/env substitution and rotate this token regularly if shared webhook endpoints are internet-reachable.

constraints.title: Diagnostics
constraints.description: Diagnostics controls for targeted tracing, telemetry export, and cache inspection during debugging. Keep baseline diagnostics minimal in production and enable deeper signals only when investigating issues.

constraints.title: Cache Trace
constraints.description: Cache-trace logging settings for observing cache decisions and payload context in embedded runs. Enable this temporarily for debugging and disable afterward to reduce sensitive log footprint.

constraints.title: Cache Trace Enabled
constraints.description: Log cache trace snapshots for embedded agent runs (default: false).

constraints.title: Cache Trace File Path
constraints.description: JSONL output path for cache trace logs (default: $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/logs/cache-trace.jsonl).

constraints.title: Cache Trace Include Messages
constraints.description: Include full message payloads in trace output (default: true).

constraints.title: Cache Trace Include Prompt
constraints.description: Include prompt text in trace output (default: true).

constraints.title: Cache Trace Include System
constraints.description: Include system prompt in trace output (default: true).

constraints.title: Diagnostics Enabled
constraints.description: Master toggle for diagnostics instrumentation output in logs and telemetry wiring paths. Keep enabled for normal observability, and disable only in tightly constrained environments.

constraints.title: Diagnostics Flags
constraints.description: Enable targeted diagnostics logs by flag (e.g. ["telegram.http"]). Supports wildcards like "telegram.*" or "*".

constraints.title: OpenTelemetry
constraints.description: OpenTelemetry export settings for traces, metrics, and logs emitted by gateway components. Use this when integrating with centralized observability backends and distributed tracing pipelines.

constraints.title: OpenTelemetry Enabled
constraints.description: Enables OpenTelemetry export pipeline for traces, metrics, and logs based on configured endpoint/protocol settings. Keep disabled unless your collector endpoint and auth are fully configured.

constraints.title: OpenTelemetry Endpoint
constraints.description: Collector endpoint URL used for OpenTelemetry export transport, including scheme and port. Use a reachable, trusted collector endpoint and monitor ingestion errors after rollout.

constraints.title: OpenTelemetry Flush Interval (ms)
constraints.description: Interval in milliseconds for periodic telemetry flush from buffers to the collector. Increase to reduce export chatter, or lower for faster visibility during active incident response.

constraints.title: OpenTelemetry Headers
constraints.description: Additional HTTP/gRPC metadata headers sent with OpenTelemetry export requests, often used for tenant auth or routing. Keep secrets in env-backed values and avoid unnecessary header sprawl.

constraints.title: OpenTelemetry Logs Enabled
constraints.description: Enable log signal export through OpenTelemetry in addition to local logging sinks. Use this when centralized log correlation is required across services and agents.

constraints.title: OpenTelemetry Metrics Enabled
constraints.description: Enable metrics signal export to the configured OpenTelemetry collector endpoint. Keep enabled for runtime health dashboards, and disable only if metric volume must be minimized.

constraints.title: OpenTelemetry Protocol
constraints.description: OTel transport protocol for telemetry export: "http/protobuf" or "grpc" depending on collector support. Use the protocol your observability backend expects to avoid dropped telemetry payloads.

constraints.title: OpenTelemetry Trace Sample Rate
constraints.description: Trace sampling rate (0-1) controlling how much trace traffic is exported to observability backends. Lower rates reduce overhead/cost, while higher rates improve debugging fidelity.

constraints.title: OpenTelemetry Service Name
constraints.description: Service name reported in telemetry resource attributes to identify this gateway instance in observability backends. Use stable names so dashboards and alerts remain consistent over deployments.

constraints.title: OpenTelemetry Traces Enabled
constraints.description: Enable trace signal export to the configured OpenTelemetry collector endpoint. Keep enabled when latency/debug tracing is needed, and disable if you only want metrics/logs.

constraints.title: Stuck Session Warning Threshold (ms)
constraints.description: Age threshold in milliseconds for emitting stuck-session warnings while a session remains in processing state. Increase for long multi-tool turns to reduce false positives; decrease for faster hang detection.

constraints.title: Discovery
constraints.description: Service discovery settings for local mDNS advertisement and optional wide-area presence signaling. Keep discovery scoped to expected networks to avoid leaking service metadata.

constraints.title: mDNS Discovery
constraints.description: mDNS discovery configuration group for local network advertisement and discovery behavior tuning. Keep minimal mode for routine LAN discovery unless extra metadata is required.

constraints.title: mDNS Discovery Mode
constraints.description: mDNS broadcast mode ("minimal" default, "full" includes cliPath/sshPort, "off" disables mDNS).

constraints.title: Wide-area Discovery
constraints.description: Wide-area discovery configuration group for exposing discovery signals beyond local-link scopes. Enable only in deployments that intentionally aggregate gateway presence across sites.

constraints.title: Wide-area Discovery Domain
constraints.description: Optional unicast DNS-SD domain for wide-area discovery, such as openclaw.internal. Use this when you intentionally publish gateway discovery beyond local mDNS scopes.

constraints.title: Wide-area Discovery Enabled
constraints.description: Enables wide-area discovery signaling when your environment needs non-local gateway discovery. Keep disabled unless cross-network discovery is operationally required.

constraints.title: Environment
