constraints.description: Environment import and override settings used to supply runtime variables to the gateway process. Use this section to control shell-env loading and explicit variable injection behavior.

constraints.title: Shell Environment Import
constraints.description: Shell environment import controls for loading variables from your login shell during startup. Keep this enabled when you depend on profile-defined secrets or PATH customizations.

constraints.title: Shell Environment Import Enabled
constraints.description: Enables loading environment variables from the user shell profile during startup initialization. Keep enabled for developer machines, or disable in locked-down service environments with explicit env management.

constraints.title: Shell Environment Import Timeout (ms)
constraints.description: Maximum time in milliseconds allowed for shell environment resolution before fallback behavior applies. Use tighter timeouts for faster startup, or increase when shell initialization is heavy.

constraints.title: Environment Variable Overrides
constraints.description: Explicit key/value environment variable overrides merged into runtime process environment for OpenClaw. Use this for deterministic env configuration instead of relying only on shell profile side effects.

constraints.title: Gateway
constraints.description: Gateway runtime surface for bind mode, auth, control UI, remote transport, and operational safety controls. Keep conservative defaults unless you intentionally expose the gateway beyond trusted local interfaces.

constraints.title: Gateway Allow x-real-ip Fallback
constraints.description: Enables x-real-ip fallback when x-forwarded-for is missing in proxy scenarios. Keep disabled unless your ingress stack requires this compatibility behavior.

constraints.title: Gateway Auth
constraints.description: Authentication policy for gateway HTTP/WebSocket access including mode, credentials, trusted-proxy behavior, and rate limiting. Keep auth enabled for every non-loopback deployment.

constraints.title: Gateway Auth Allow Tailscale Identity
constraints.description: Allows trusted Tailscale identity paths to satisfy gateway auth checks when configured. Use this only when your tailnet identity posture is strong and operator workflows depend on it.

constraints.title: Gateway Auth Mode
constraints.description: Gateway auth mode: "none", "token", "password", or "trusted-proxy" depending on your edge architecture. Use token/password for direct exposure, and trusted-proxy only behind hardened identity-aware proxies.

constraints.title: Gateway Password
constraints.description: Required for Tailscale funnel.

constraints.title: Gateway Auth Rate Limit
constraints.description: Login/auth attempt throttling controls to reduce credential brute-force risk at the gateway boundary. Keep enabled in exposed environments and tune thresholds to your traffic baseline.

constraints.title: Gateway Token
constraints.description: Required by default for gateway access (unless using Tailscale Serve identity); required for non-loopback binds.

constraints.title: Gateway Trusted Proxy Auth
constraints.description: Trusted-proxy auth header mapping for upstream identity providers that inject user claims. Use only with known proxy CIDRs and strict header allowlists to prevent spoofed identity headers.

constraints.title: Gateway Bind Mode
constraints.description: Network bind profile: "auto", "lan", "loopback", "custom", or "tailnet" to control interface exposure. Keep "loopback" or "auto" for safest local operation unless external clients must connect.

constraints.title: Gateway Channel Health Check Interval (min)
constraints.description: Interval in minutes for automatic channel health probing and status updates. Use lower intervals for faster detection, or higher intervals to reduce periodic probe noise.

constraints.title: Gateway Channel Max Restarts Per Hour
constraints.description: Maximum number of health-monitor-initiated channel restarts allowed within a rolling one-hour window. Once hit, further restarts are skipped until the window expires. Default: 10.

constraints.title: Gateway Channel Stale Event Threshold (min)
constraints.description: How many minutes a connected channel can go without receiving any event before the health monitor treats it as a stale socket and triggers a restart. Default: 30.

constraints.title: Control UI
constraints.description: Control UI hosting settings including enablement, pathing, and browser-origin/auth hardening behavior. Keep UI exposure minimal and pair with strong auth controls before internet-facing deployments.

constraints.title: Allow External Control UI Embed URLs
constraints.description: DANGEROUS toggle that allows hosted embeds to load absolute external http(s) URLs. Keep this off unless your Control UI intentionally embeds trusted third-party pages; hosted /__openclaw__/canvas and /__openclaw__/a2ui documents do not need it.

constraints.title: Insecure Control UI Auth Toggle
constraints.description: Loosens strict browser auth checks for Control UI when you must run a non-standard setup. Keep this off unless you trust your network and proxy path, because impersonation risk is higher.

constraints.title: Control UI Allowed Origins
constraints.description: Allowed browser origins for Control UI/WebChat websocket connections (full origins only, e.g. https://control.example.com). Required for non-loopback Control UI deployments unless dangerous Host-header fallback is explicitly enabled. Setting ["*"] means allow any browser origin and should be avoided outside tightly controlled local testing.

constraints.title: Control UI Base Path
constraints.description: Optional URL prefix where the Control UI is served (e.g. /openclaw).

constraints.title: Dangerously Allow Host-Header Origin Fallback
constraints.description: DANGEROUS toggle that enables Host-header based origin fallback for Control UI/WebChat websocket checks. This mode is supported when your deployment intentionally relies on Host-header origin policy; explicit gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins remains the recommended hardened default.

constraints.title: Dangerously Disable Control UI Device Auth
constraints.description: Disables Control UI device identity checks and relies on token/password only. Use only for short-lived debugging on trusted networks, then turn it off immediately.

constraints.title: Control UI Embed Sandbox Mode
constraints.description: Iframe sandbox policy for hosted Control UI embeds. "strict" disables scripts, "scripts" allows interactive embeds while keeping origin isolation (default), and "trusted" adds `allow-same-origin` for same-site documents that intentionally need stronger privileges.

constraints.title: Control UI Enabled
constraints.description: Enables serving the gateway Control UI from the gateway HTTP process when true. Keep enabled for local administration, and disable when an external control surface replaces it.

constraints.title: Control UI Assets Root
constraints.description: Optional filesystem root for Control UI assets (defaults to dist/control-ui).

constraints.title: Gateway Custom Bind Host
constraints.description: Explicit bind host/IP used when gateway.bind is set to custom for manual interface targeting. Use a precise address and avoid wildcard binds unless external exposure is required.

constraints.title: Gateway HTTP API
constraints.description: Gateway HTTP API configuration grouping endpoint toggles and transport-facing API exposure controls. Keep only required endpoints enabled to reduce attack surface.

constraints.title: Gateway HTTP Endpoints
constraints.description: HTTP endpoint feature toggles under the gateway API surface for compatibility routes and optional integrations. Enable endpoints intentionally and monitor access patterns after rollout.

constraints.title: OpenAI Chat Completions Endpoint
constraints.description: Enable the OpenAI-compatible `POST /v1/chat/completions` endpoint (default: false).

constraints.title: OpenAI Chat Completions Image Limits
constraints.description: Image fetch/validation controls for OpenAI-compatible `image_url` parts.

constraints.title: OpenAI Chat Completions Allow Image URLs
constraints.description: Allow server-side URL fetches for `image_url` parts (default: false; data URIs remain supported). Set this to `false` to disable URL fetching entirely.

constraints.title: OpenAI Chat Completions Image MIME Allowlist
constraints.description: Allowed MIME types for `image_url` parts (case-insensitive list).

constraints.title: OpenAI Chat Completions Image Max Bytes
constraints.description: Max bytes per fetched/decoded `image_url` image (default: 10MB).

constraints.title: OpenAI Chat Completions Image Max Redirects
constraints.description: Max HTTP redirects allowed when fetching `image_url` URLs (default: 3).

constraints.title: OpenAI Chat Completions Image Timeout (ms)
constraints.description: Timeout in milliseconds for `image_url` URL fetches (default: 10000).

constraints.title: OpenAI Chat Completions Image URL Allowlist
constraints.description: Optional hostname allowlist for `image_url` URL fetches; supports exact hosts and `*.example.com` wildcards. Empty or omitted lists mean no hostname allowlist restriction.

constraints.title: OpenAI Chat Completions Max Body Bytes
constraints.description: Max request body size in bytes for `/v1/chat/completions` (default: 20MB).

constraints.title: OpenAI Chat Completions Max Image Parts
constraints.description: Max number of `image_url` parts accepted from the latest user message (default: 8).

constraints.title: OpenAI Chat Completions Max Total Image Bytes
constraints.description: Max cumulative decoded bytes across all `image_url` parts in one request (default: 20MB).

constraints.title: Gateway HTTP Security Headers
constraints.description: Optional HTTP response security headers applied by the gateway process itself. Prefer setting these at your reverse proxy when TLS terminates there.

constraints.title: Strict Transport Security Header
constraints.description: Value for the Strict-Transport-Security response header. Set only on HTTPS origins that you fully control; use false to explicitly disable.

constraints.title: Gateway Mode
constraints.description: Gateway operation mode: "local" runs channels and agent runtime on this host, while "remote" connects through remote transport. Keep "local" unless you intentionally run a split remote gateway topology.

constraints.title: Gateway Node Allowlist (Extra Commands)
constraints.description: Extra node.invoke commands to allow beyond the gateway defaults (array of command strings). Enabling dangerous commands here is a security-sensitive override and is flagged by `openclaw security audit`.

constraints.title: Gateway Node Browser Mode
constraints.description: Node browser routing ("auto" = pick single connected browser node, "manual" = require node param, "off" = disable).

constraints.title: Gateway Node Browser Pin
constraints.description: Pin browser routing to a specific node id or name (optional).

constraints.title: Gateway Node Denylist
constraints.description: Node command names to block even if present in node claims or default allowlist (exact command-name matching only, e.g. `system.run`; does not inspect shell text inside that command).

constraints.title: Gateway Port
constraints.description: TCP port used by the gateway listener for API, control UI, and channel-facing ingress paths. Use a dedicated port and avoid collisions with reverse proxies or local developer services.

constraints.title: Gateway Push Delivery
constraints.description: Push-delivery settings used by the gateway when it needs to wake or notify paired devices. Configure relay-backed APNs here for official iOS builds; direct APNs auth remains env-based for local/manual builds.

constraints.title: Gateway APNs Delivery
constraints.description: APNs delivery settings for iOS devices paired to this gateway. Use relay settings for official/TestFlight builds that register through the external push relay.

constraints.title: Gateway APNs Relay
constraints.description: External relay settings for relay-backed APNs sends. The gateway uses this relay for push.test, wake nudges, and reconnect wakes after a paired official iOS build publishes a relay-backed registration.

constraints.title: Gateway APNs Relay Base URL
constraints.description: Base HTTPS URL for the external APNs relay service used by official/TestFlight iOS builds. Keep this aligned with the relay URL baked into the iOS build so registration and send traffic hit the same deployment.

constraints.title: Gateway APNs Relay Timeout (ms)
constraints.description: Timeout in milliseconds for relay send requests from the gateway to the APNs relay (default: 10000). Increase for slower relays or networks, or lower to fail wake attempts faster.

constraints.title: Config Reload
constraints.description: Live config-reload policy for how edits are applied and when full restarts are triggered. Keep hybrid behavior for safest operational updates unless debugging reload internals.

constraints.title: Config Reload Debounce (ms)
constraints.description: Debounce window (ms) before applying config changes.

constraints.title: Restart Deferral Timeout (ms)
constraints.description: Maximum time (ms) to wait for in-flight operations to complete before forcing a SIGUSR1 restart. Default: 300000 (5 minutes). Lower values risk aborting active subagent LLM calls.

constraints.title: Config Reload Mode
constraints.description: Controls how config edits are applied: "off" ignores live edits, "restart" always restarts, "hot" applies in-process, and "hybrid" tries hot then restarts if required. Keep "hybrid" for safest routine updates.

constraints.title: Remote Gateway
constraints.description: Remote gateway connection settings for direct or SSH transport when this instance proxies to another runtime host. Use remote mode only when split-host operation is intentionally configured.

constraints.title: Remote Gateway Password
constraints.description: Password credential used for remote gateway authentication when password mode is enabled. Keep this secret managed externally and avoid plaintext values in committed config.

constraints.title: Remote Gateway SSH Identity
constraints.description: Optional SSH identity file path (passed to ssh -i).

constraints.title: Remote Gateway SSH Target
constraints.description: Remote gateway over SSH (tunnels the gateway port to localhost). Format: user@host or user@host:port.

constraints.title: Remote Gateway TLS Fingerprint
constraints.description: Expected sha256 TLS fingerprint for the remote gateway (pin to avoid MITM).

constraints.title: Remote Gateway Token
constraints.description: Bearer token used to authenticate this client to a remote gateway in token-auth deployments. Store via secret/env substitution and rotate alongside remote gateway auth changes.

constraints.title: Remote Gateway Transport
constraints.description: Remote connection transport: "direct" uses configured URL connectivity, while "ssh" tunnels through SSH. Use SSH when you need encrypted tunnel semantics without exposing remote ports.

constraints.title: Remote Gateway URL
constraints.description: Remote Gateway WebSocket URL (ws:// or wss://).

constraints.title: Gateway Tailscale
constraints.description: Tailscale integration settings for Serve/Funnel exposure and lifecycle handling on gateway start/exit. Keep off unless your deployment intentionally relies on Tailscale ingress.

constraints.title: Gateway Tailscale Mode
constraints.description: Tailscale publish mode: "off", "serve", or "funnel" for private or public exposure paths. Use "serve" for tailnet-only access and "funnel" only when public internet reachability is required.

constraints.title: Gateway Tailscale Reset on Exit
constraints.description: Resets Tailscale Serve/Funnel state on gateway exit to avoid stale published routes after shutdown. Keep enabled unless another controller manages publish lifecycle outside the gateway.

constraints.title: Gateway TLS
constraints.description: TLS certificate and key settings for terminating HTTPS directly in the gateway process. Use explicit certificates in production and avoid plaintext exposure on untrusted networks.

constraints.title: Gateway TLS Auto-Generate Cert
constraints.description: Auto-generates a local TLS certificate/key pair when explicit files are not configured. Use only for local/dev setups and replace with real certificates for production traffic.

constraints.title: Gateway TLS CA Path
constraints.description: Optional CA bundle path for client verification or custom trust-chain requirements at the gateway edge. Use this when private PKI or custom certificate chains are part of deployment.

constraints.title: Gateway TLS Certificate Path
constraints.description: Filesystem path to the TLS certificate file used by the gateway when TLS is enabled. Use managed certificate paths and keep renewal automation aligned with this location.

constraints.title: Gateway TLS Enabled
constraints.description: Enables TLS termination at the gateway listener so clients connect over HTTPS/WSS directly. Keep enabled for direct internet exposure or any untrusted network boundary.

constraints.title: Gateway TLS Key Path
constraints.description: Filesystem path to the TLS private key file used by the gateway when TLS is enabled. Keep this key file permission-restricted and rotate per your security policy.

constraints.title: Gateway Tool Exposure Policy
constraints.description: Gateway-level tool exposure allow/deny policy that can restrict runtime tool availability independent of agent/tool profiles. Use this for coarse emergency controls and production hardening.

constraints.title: Gateway Tool Allowlist
constraints.description: Explicit gateway-level tool allowlist when you want a narrow set of tools available at runtime. Use this for locked-down environments where tool scope must be tightly controlled.

constraints.title: Gateway Tool Denylist
constraints.description: Explicit gateway-level tool denylist to block risky tools even if lower-level policies allow them. Use deny rules for emergency response and defense-in-depth hardening.

constraints.title: Gateway Trusted Proxy CIDRs
constraints.description: CIDR/IP allowlist of upstream proxies permitted to provide forwarded client identity headers. Keep this list narrow so untrusted hops cannot impersonate users.

constraints.title: WebChat History Max Chars
constraints.description: Max characters per text field in chat.history responses before truncation (default: 12000).

constraints.title: Hooks
constraints.description: Inbound webhook automation surface for mapping external events into wake or agent actions in OpenClaw. Keep this locked down with explicit token/session/agent controls before exposing it beyond trusted networks.

constraints.title: Hooks Allow Request Session Key
constraints.description: Allows callers to supply a session key in hook requests when true, enabling caller-controlled routing. Keep false unless trusted integrators explicitly need custom session threading.

constraints.title: Hooks Allowed Agent IDs
constraints.description: Allowlist of agent IDs that hook mappings are allowed to target when selecting execution agents. Use this to constrain automation events to dedicated service agents and reduce blast radius if a hook token is exposed.

constraints.title: Hooks Allowed Session Key Prefixes
constraints.description: Allowlist of accepted session-key prefixes for inbound hook requests when caller-provided keys are enabled. Use narrow prefixes to prevent arbitrary session-key injection.

constraints.title: Hooks Default Session Key
constraints.description: Fallback session key used for hook deliveries when a request does not provide one through allowed channels. Use a stable but scoped key to avoid mixing unrelated automation conversations.

constraints.title: Hooks Enabled
constraints.description: Enables the hooks endpoint and mapping execution pipeline for inbound webhook requests. Keep disabled unless you are actively routing external events into the gateway.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook
constraints.description: Gmail push integration settings used for Pub/Sub notifications and optional local callback serving. Keep this scoped to dedicated Gmail automation accounts where possible.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook Account
constraints.description: Google account identifier used for Gmail watch/subscription operations in this hook integration. Use a dedicated automation mailbox account to isolate operational permissions.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook Allow Unsafe External Content
constraints.description: Allows less-sanitized external Gmail content to pass into processing when enabled. Keep disabled for safer defaults, and enable only for trusted mail streams with controlled transforms.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook Callback URL
constraints.description: Public callback URL Gmail or intermediaries invoke to deliver notifications into this hook pipeline. Keep this URL protected with token validation and restricted network exposure.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook Include Body
constraints.description: When true, fetch and include email body content for downstream mapping/agent processing. Keep false unless body text is required, because this increases payload size and sensitivity.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook Label
constraints.description: Optional Gmail label filter limiting which labeled messages trigger hook events. Keep filters narrow to avoid flooding automations with unrelated inbox traffic.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook Max Body Bytes
constraints.description: Maximum Gmail payload bytes processed per event when includeBody is enabled. Keep conservative limits to reduce oversized message processing cost and risk.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook Model Override
constraints.description: Optional model override for Gmail-triggered runs when mailbox automations should use dedicated model behavior. Keep unset to inherit agent defaults unless mailbox tasks need specialization.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook Push Token
constraints.description: Shared secret token required on Gmail push hook callbacks before processing notifications. Use env substitution and rotate if callback endpoints are exposed externally.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook Renew Interval (min)
constraints.description: Renewal cadence in minutes for Gmail watch subscriptions to prevent expiration. Set below provider expiration windows and monitor renew failures in logs.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook Local Server
constraints.description: Local callback server settings block for directly receiving Gmail notifications without a separate ingress layer. Enable only when this process should terminate webhook traffic itself.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook Server Bind Address
constraints.description: Bind address for the local Gmail callback HTTP server used when serving hooks directly. Keep loopback-only unless external ingress is intentionally required.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook Server Path
constraints.description: HTTP path on the local Gmail callback server where push notifications are accepted. Keep this consistent with subscription configuration to avoid dropped events.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook Server Port
constraints.description: Port for the local Gmail callback HTTP server when serve mode is enabled. Use a dedicated port to avoid collisions with gateway/control interfaces.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook Subscription
constraints.description: Pub/Sub subscription consumed by the gateway to receive Gmail change notifications from the configured topic. Keep subscription ownership clear so multiple consumers do not race unexpectedly.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook Tailscale
constraints.description: Tailscale exposure configuration block for publishing Gmail callbacks through Serve/Funnel routes. Use private tailnet modes before enabling any public ingress path.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook Tailscale Mode
constraints.description: Tailscale exposure mode for Gmail callbacks: "off", "serve", or "funnel". Use "serve" for private tailnet delivery and "funnel" only when public internet ingress is required.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook Tailscale Path
constraints.description: Path published by Tailscale Serve/Funnel for Gmail callback forwarding when enabled. Keep it aligned with Gmail webhook config so requests reach the expected handler.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook Tailscale Target
constraints.description: Local service target forwarded by Tailscale Serve/Funnel (for example http://127.0.0.1:8787). Use explicit loopback targets to avoid ambiguous routing.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook Thinking Override
constraints.description: Thinking effort override for Gmail-driven agent runs: "off", "minimal", "low", "medium", or "high". Keep modest defaults for routine inbox automations to control cost and latency.

constraints.title: Gmail Hook Pub/Sub Topic
constraints.description: Google Pub/Sub topic name used by Gmail watch to publish change notifications for this account. Ensure the topic IAM grants Gmail publish access before enabling watches.

constraints.title: Internal Hooks
constraints.description: Internal hook runtime settings for bundled/custom event handlers loaded from module paths. Use this for trusted in-process automations and keep handler loading tightly scoped.

constraints.title: Internal Hooks Enabled
constraints.description: Enables processing for internal hooks and configured entries in the internal hook runtime. Keep disabled unless internal hooks are intentionally configured.

constraints.title: Internal Hook Entries
constraints.description: Configured internal hook entry records used to register concrete runtime handlers and metadata. Keep entries explicit and versioned so production behavior is auditable.

constraints.title: Internal Hook Install Records
constraints.description: Install metadata for internal hook modules, including source and resolved artifacts for repeatable deployments. Use this as operational provenance and avoid manual drift edits.

constraints.title: Internal Hook Loader
constraints.description: Internal hook loader settings controlling where handler modules are discovered at startup. Use constrained load roots to reduce accidental module conflicts or shadowing.

constraints.title: Internal Hook Extra Directories
constraints.description: Additional directories searched for internal hook modules beyond default load paths. Keep this minimal and controlled to reduce accidental module shadowing.

id.title: Hook Mapping ID
id.description: Optional stable identifier for a hook mapping entry used for auditing, troubleshooting, and targeted updates. Use unique IDs so logs and config diffs can reference mappings unambiguously.

path.title: Hook Mapping Match Path
path.description: Path match condition for a hook mapping, usually compared against the inbound request path. Use this to split automation behavior by webhook endpoint path families.

source.title: Hook Mapping Match Source
source.description: Source match condition for a hook mapping, typically set by trusted upstream metadata or adapter logic. Use stable source identifiers so routing remains deterministic across retries.

match.title: Hook Mapping Match
match.description: Grouping object for mapping match predicates such as path and source before action routing is applied. Keep match criteria specific so unrelated webhook traffic does not trigger automations.

action.title: Hook Mapping Action
action.description: Mapping action type: "wake" triggers agent wake flow, while "agent" sends directly to agent handling. Use "agent" for immediate execution and "wake" when heartbeat-driven processing is preferred.

wakeMode.title: Hook Mapping Wake Mode
wakeMode.description: Wake scheduling mode: "now" wakes immediately, while "next-heartbeat" defers until the next heartbeat cycle. Use deferred mode for lower-priority automations that can tolerate slight delay.

name.title: Hook Mapping Name
name.description: Human-readable mapping display name used in diagnostics and operator-facing config UIs. Keep names concise and descriptive so routing intent is obvious during incident review.

agentId.title: Hook Mapping Agent ID
agentId.description: Target agent ID for mapping execution when action routing should not use defaults. Use dedicated automation agents to isolate webhook behavior from interactive operator sessions.

sessionKey.title: Hook Mapping Session Key
sessionKey.description: Explicit session key override for mapping-delivered messages to control thread continuity. Use stable scoped keys so repeated events correlate without leaking into unrelated conversations.

messageTemplate.title: Hook Mapping Message Template
messageTemplate.description: Template for synthesizing structured mapping input into the final message content sent to the target action path. Keep templates deterministic so downstream parsing and behavior remain stable.

textTemplate.title: Hook Mapping Text Template
textTemplate.description: Text-only fallback template used when rich payload rendering is not desired or not supported. Use this to provide a concise, consistent summary string for chat delivery surfaces.

deliver.title: Hook Mapping Deliver Reply
deliver.description: Controls whether mapping execution results are delivered back to a channel destination versus being processed silently. Disable delivery for background automations that should not post user-facing output.

allowUnsafeExternalContent.title: Hook Mapping Allow Unsafe External Content
allowUnsafeExternalContent.description: When true, mapping content may include less-sanitized external payload data in generated messages. Keep false by default and enable only for trusted sources with reviewed transform logic.

channel.title: Hook Mapping Delivery Channel
channel.description: Delivery channel override for mapping outputs (for example "last", "telegram", "discord", "slack", "signal", "imessage", or "msteams"). Keep channel overrides explicit to avoid accidental cross-channel sends.

to.title: Hook Mapping Delivery Destination
to.description: Destination identifier inside the selected channel when mapping replies should route to a fixed target. Verify provider-specific destination formats before enabling production mappings.

model.title: Hook Mapping Model Override
model.description: Optional model override for mapping-triggered runs when automation should use a different model than agent defaults. Use this sparingly so behavior remains predictable across mapping executions.

thinking.title: Hook Mapping Thinking Override
thinking.description: Optional thinking-effort override for mapping-triggered runs to tune latency versus reasoning depth. Keep low or minimal for high-volume hooks unless deeper reasoning is clearly required.

timeoutSeconds.title: Hook Mapping Timeout (sec)
timeoutSeconds.description: Maximum runtime allowed for mapping action execution before timeout handling applies. Use tighter limits for high-volume webhook sources to prevent queue pileups.

module.title: Hook Transform Module
module.description: Relative transform module path loaded from hooks.transformsDir to rewrite incoming payloads before delivery. Keep modules local, reviewed, and free of path traversal patterns.

export.title: Hook Transform Export
export.description: Named export to invoke from the transform module; defaults to module default export when omitted. Set this when one file hosts multiple transform handlers.

transform.title: Hook Mapping Transform
transform.description: Transform configuration block defining module/export preprocessing before mapping action handling. Use transforms only from reviewed code paths and keep behavior deterministic for repeatable automation.

constraints.title: Hook Mappings
constraints.description: Ordered mapping rules that match inbound hook requests and choose wake or agent actions with optional delivery routing. Use specific mappings first to avoid broad pattern rules capturing everything.

constraints.title: Hooks Max Body Bytes
constraints.description: Maximum accepted webhook payload size in bytes before the request is rejected. Keep this bounded to reduce abuse risk and protect memory usage under bursty integrations.

constraints.title: Hooks Endpoint Path
constraints.description: HTTP path used by the hooks endpoint (for example `/hooks`) on the gateway control server. Use a non-guessable path and combine it with token validation for defense in depth.

constraints.title: Hooks Presets
constraints.description: Named hook preset bundles applied at load time to seed standard mappings and behavior defaults. Keep preset usage explicit so operators can audit which automations are active.

constraints.title: Hooks Auth Token
constraints.description: Shared bearer token checked by hooks ingress for request authentication before mappings run. Treat holders as full-trust callers for the hook ingress surface, not as a separate non-owner role. Use environment substitution and rotate regularly when webhook endpoints are internet-accessible.

constraints.title: Hooks Transforms Directory
constraints.description: Base directory for hook transform modules referenced by mapping transform.module paths. Use a controlled repo directory so dynamic imports remain reviewable and predictable.

constraints.title: Logging
constraints.description: Logging behavior controls for severity, output destinations, formatting, and sensitive-data redaction. Keep levels and redaction strict enough for production while preserving useful diagnostics.

constraints.title: Console Log Level
constraints.description: Console-specific log threshold: "silent", "fatal", "error", "warn", "info", "debug", or "trace" for terminal output control. Use this to keep local console quieter while retaining richer file logging if needed.

constraints.title: Console Log Style
constraints.description: Console output format style: "pretty", "compact", or "json" based on operator and ingestion needs. Use json for machine parsing pipelines and pretty/compact for human-first terminal workflows.

constraints.title: Log File Path
constraints.description: Optional file path for persisted log output in addition to or instead of console logging. Use a managed writable path and align retention/rotation with your operational policy.

constraints.title: Log Level
constraints.description: Primary log level threshold for runtime logger output: "silent", "fatal", "error", "warn", "info", "debug", or "trace". Keep "info" or "warn" for production, and use debug/trace only during investigation.

constraints.title: Custom Redaction Patterns
constraints.description: Additional custom redact regex patterns applied to log output before emission/storage. Use this to mask org-specific tokens and identifiers not covered by built-in redaction rules.

constraints.title: Sensitive Data Redaction Mode
constraints.description: Sensitive redaction mode: "off" disables built-in masking, while "tools" redacts sensitive tool/config payload fields. Keep "tools" in shared logs unless you have isolated secure log sinks.

constraints.title: MCP
constraints.description: Global MCP server definitions managed by OpenClaw. Embedded Pi and other runtime adapters can consume these servers without storing them inside Pi-owned project settings.

constraints.title: MCP Servers
constraints.description: Named MCP server definitions. OpenClaw stores them in its own config and runtime adapters decide which transports are supported at execution time.

constraints.title: Media
constraints.description: Top-level media behavior shared across providers and tools that handle inbound files. Keep defaults unless you need stable filenames for external processing pipelines or longer-lived inbound media retention.

constraints.title: Preserve Media Filenames
constraints.description: When enabled, uploaded media keeps its original filename instead of a generated temp-safe name. Turn this on when downstream automations depend on stable names, and leave off to reduce accidental filename leakage.

constraints.title: Media Retention TTL (hours)
constraints.description: Optional retention window in hours for persisted inbound media cleanup across the full media tree. Leave unset to preserve legacy behavior, or set values like 24 (1 day) or 168 (7 days) when you want automatic cleanup.

constraints.title: Memory
constraints.description: Memory backend configuration (global).

constraints.title: Memory Backend
constraints.description: Selects the global memory engine: "builtin" uses OpenClaw memory internals, while "qmd" uses the QMD sidecar pipeline. Keep "builtin" unless you intentionally operate QMD.

constraints.title: Memory Citations Mode
constraints.description: Controls citation visibility in replies: "auto" shows citations when useful, "on" always shows them, and "off" hides them. Keep "auto" for a balanced signal-to-noise default.

constraints.title: QMD Binary
constraints.description: Sets the executable path for the `qmd` binary used by the QMD backend (default: resolved from PATH). Use an explicit absolute path when multiple qmd installs exist or PATH differs across environments.

constraints.title: QMD Include Default Memory
constraints.description: Automatically indexes default memory files (MEMORY.md and memory/**/*.md) into QMD collections. Keep enabled unless you want indexing controlled only through explicit custom paths.

constraints.title: QMD Max Injected Chars
constraints.description: Caps how much QMD text can be injected into one turn across all hits. Use lower values to control prompt bloat and latency; raise only when context is consistently truncated.

constraints.title: QMD Max Results
constraints.description: Limits how many QMD hits are returned into the agent loop for each recall request (default: 6). Increase for broader recall context, or lower to keep prompts tighter and faster.

constraints.title: QMD Max Snippet Chars
constraints.description: Caps per-result snippet length extracted from QMD hits in characters (default: 700). Lower this when prompts bloat quickly, and raise only if answers consistently miss key details.

constraints.title: QMD Search Timeout (ms)
constraints.description: Sets per-query QMD search timeout in milliseconds (default: 4000). Increase for larger indexes or slower environments, and lower to keep request latency bounded.

constraints.title: QMD MCPorter
constraints.description: Routes QMD work through mcporter (MCP runtime) instead of spawning `qmd` for each call. Use this when cold starts are expensive on large models; keep direct process mode for simpler local setups.

constraints.title: QMD MCPorter Enabled
constraints.description: Routes QMD through an mcporter daemon instead of spawning qmd per request, reducing cold-start overhead for larger models. Keep disabled unless mcporter is installed and configured.

constraints.title: QMD MCPorter Server Name
constraints.description: Names the mcporter server target used for QMD calls (default: qmd). Change only when your mcporter setup uses a custom server name for qmd mcp keep-alive.

constraints.title: QMD MCPorter Start Daemon
constraints.description: Automatically starts the mcporter daemon when mcporter-backed QMD mode is enabled (default: true). Keep enabled unless process lifecycle is managed externally by your service supervisor.

constraints.title: QMD Extra Paths
constraints.description: Adds custom directories or files to include in QMD indexing, each with an optional name and glob pattern. Use this for project-specific knowledge locations that are outside default memory paths.

constraints.title: QMD Surface Scope
constraints.description: Defines which sessions/channels are eligible for QMD recall using session.sendPolicy-style rules. Keep default direct-only scope unless you intentionally want cross-chat memory sharing.

constraints.title: QMD Search Mode
constraints.description: Selects the QMD retrieval path: "query" uses standard query flow, "search" uses search-oriented retrieval, and "vsearch" emphasizes vector retrieval. Keep default unless tuning relevance quality.

constraints.title: QMD Search Tool Override
constraints.description: Overrides the exact mcporter tool name used for QMD searches while preserving `searchMode` as the semantic retrieval mode. Use this only when your QMD MCP server exposes a custom tool such as `hybrid_search` and keep it unset for the normal built-in tool mapping.

constraints.title: QMD Session Indexing
constraints.description: Indexes session transcripts into QMD so recall can include prior conversation content (experimental, default: false). Enable only when transcript memory is required and you accept larger index churn.

constraints.title: QMD Session Export Directory
constraints.description: Overrides where sanitized session exports are written before QMD indexing. Use this when default state storage is constrained or when exports must land on a managed volume.

constraints.title: QMD Session Retention (days)
constraints.description: Defines how long exported session files are kept before automatic pruning, in days (default: unlimited). Set a finite value for storage hygiene or compliance retention policies.

constraints.title: QMD Command Timeout (ms)
constraints.description: Sets timeout for QMD maintenance commands such as collection list/add in milliseconds (default: 30000). Increase when running on slower disks or remote filesystems that delay command completion.

constraints.title: QMD Update Debounce (ms)
constraints.description: Sets the minimum delay between consecutive QMD refresh attempts in milliseconds (default: 15000). Increase this if frequent file changes cause update thrash or unnecessary background load.

constraints.title: QMD Embed Interval
constraints.description: Sets how often QMD recomputes embeddings (duration string, default: 60m; set 0 to disable periodic embeds). Lower intervals improve freshness but increase embedding workload and cost.

constraints.title: QMD Embed Timeout (ms)
constraints.description: Sets maximum runtime for each `qmd embed` cycle in milliseconds (default: 120000). Increase for heavier embedding workloads or slower hardware, and lower to fail fast under tight SLAs.

constraints.title: QMD Update Interval
constraints.description: Sets how often QMD refreshes indexes from source content (duration string, default: 5m). Shorter intervals improve freshness but increase background CPU and I/O.

constraints.title: QMD Update on Startup
constraints.description: Runs an initial QMD update once during gateway startup (default: true). Keep enabled so recall starts from a fresh baseline; disable only when startup speed is more important than immediate freshness.

constraints.title: QMD Update Timeout (ms)
constraints.description: Sets maximum runtime for each `qmd update` cycle in milliseconds (default: 120000). Raise this for larger collections; lower it when you want quicker failure detection in automation.

constraints.title: QMD Wait for Boot Sync
constraints.description: Blocks startup completion until the initial boot-time QMD sync finishes (default: false). Enable when you need fully up-to-date recall before serving traffic, and keep off for faster boot.

constraints.title: Messages
constraints.description: Message formatting, acknowledgment, queueing, debounce, and status reaction behavior for inbound/outbound chat flows. Use this section when channel responsiveness or message UX needs adjustment.

constraints.title: Ack Reaction Emoji
constraints.description: Emoji reaction used to acknowledge inbound messages (empty disables).

constraints.title: Ack Reaction Scope
constraints.description: When to send ack reactions ("group-mentions", "group-all", "direct", "all", "off", "none"). "off"/"none" disables ack reactions entirely.

constraints.title: Group Chat Rules
constraints.description: Group-message handling controls including mention triggers and history window sizing. Keep mention patterns narrow so group channels do not trigger on every message.

constraints.title: Group History Limit
constraints.description: Maximum number of prior group messages loaded as context per turn for group sessions. Use higher values for richer continuity, or lower values for faster and cheaper responses.

constraints.title: Group Mention Patterns
constraints.description: Safe case-insensitive regex patterns used to detect explicit mentions/trigger phrases in group chats. Use precise patterns to reduce false positives in high-volume channels; invalid or unsafe nested-repetition patterns are ignored.

constraints.title: Inbound Debounce
constraints.description: Direct inbound debounce settings used before queue/turn processing starts. Configure this for provider-specific rapid message bursts from the same sender.

constraints.title: Inbound Debounce by Channel (ms)
constraints.description: Per-channel inbound debounce overrides keyed by provider id in milliseconds. Use this where some providers send message fragments more aggressively than others.

constraints.title: Inbound Message Debounce (ms)
constraints.description: Debounce window (ms) for batching rapid inbound messages from the same sender (0 to disable).

constraints.title: Inbound Message Prefix
constraints.description: Prefix text prepended to inbound user messages before they are handed to the agent runtime. Use this sparingly for channel context markers and keep it stable across sessions.

constraints.title: Inbound Queue
constraints.description: Inbound message queue strategy used to buffer bursts before processing turns. Tune this for busy channels where sequential processing or batching behavior matters.

constraints.title: Queue Mode by Channel
constraints.description: Per-channel queue mode overrides keyed by provider id (for example telegram, discord, slack). Use this when one channel’s traffic pattern needs different queue behavior than global defaults.

constraints.title: Queue Capacity
constraints.description: Maximum number of queued inbound items retained before drop policy applies. Keep caps bounded in noisy channels so memory usage remains predictable.

constraints.title: Queue Debounce (ms)
constraints.description: Global queue debounce window in milliseconds before processing buffered inbound messages. Use higher values to coalesce rapid bursts, or lower values for reduced response latency.

constraints.title: Queue Debounce by Channel (ms)
constraints.description: Per-channel debounce overrides for queue behavior keyed by provider id. Use this to tune burst handling independently for chat surfaces with different pacing.

constraints.title: Queue Drop Strategy
constraints.description: Drop strategy when queue cap is exceeded: "old", "new", or "summarize". Use summarize when preserving intent matters, or old/new when deterministic dropping is preferred.

constraints.title: Queue Mode
constraints.description: Queue behavior mode: "steer", "followup", "collect", "steer-backlog", "steer+backlog", "queue", or "interrupt". Keep conservative modes unless you intentionally need aggressive interruption/backlog semantics.

constraints.title: Remove Ack Reaction After Reply
constraints.description: Removes the acknowledgment reaction after final reply delivery when enabled. Keep enabled for cleaner UX in channels where persistent ack reactions create clutter.

constraints.title: Outbound Response Prefix
constraints.description: Prefix text prepended to outbound assistant replies before sending to channels. Use for lightweight branding/context tags and avoid long prefixes that reduce content density.

constraints.title: Status Reactions
constraints.description: Lifecycle status reactions that update the emoji on the trigger message as the agent progresses (queued → thinking → tool → done/error).

constraints.title: Status Reaction Emojis
constraints.description: Override default status reaction emojis. Keys: thinking, compacting, tool, coding, web, done, error, stallSoft, stallHard. Must be valid Telegram reaction emojis.

constraints.title: Enable Status Reactions
constraints.description: Enable lifecycle status reactions on supported channels. Slack and Discord treat unset as enabled when ack reactions are active; Telegram requires this to be true before lifecycle reactions are used.

constraints.title: Status Reaction Timing
constraints.description: Override default timing. Keys: debounceMs (700), stallSoftMs (25000), stallHardMs (60000), doneHoldMs (1500), errorHoldMs (2500).

constraints.title: Suppress Tool Error Warnings
constraints.description: When true, suppress ⚠️ tool-error warnings from being shown to the user. The agent already sees errors in context and can retry. Default: false.

constraints.title: Message Text-to-Speech
constraints.description: Text-to-speech policy for reading agent replies aloud on supported voice or audio surfaces. Keep disabled unless voice playback is part of your operator/user workflow.

apiKey.title: TTS Provider API Key
apiKey.description: Provider API key used by that speech provider when its plugin requires authenticated TTS access.

additionalProperties.title: TTS Provider Config
additionalProperties.description: Provider-specific TTS configuration for one speech provider id. Keep fields scoped to the plugin that owns that provider.

constraints.title: TTS Provider Settings
constraints.description: Provider-specific TTS settings keyed by speech provider id. Use this instead of bundled provider-specific top-level keys so speech plugins stay decoupled from core config schema.

constraints.title: Metadata
constraints.description: Metadata fields automatically maintained by OpenClaw to record write/version history for this config file. Keep these values system-managed and avoid manual edits unless debugging migration history.

constraints.title: Config Last Touched At
constraints.description: ISO timestamp of the last config write (auto-set).

constraints.title: Config Last Touched Version
constraints.description: Auto-set when OpenClaw writes the config.

constraints.title: Models
constraints.description: Model catalog root for provider definitions, merge/replace behavior, and optional Bedrock discovery integration. Keep provider definitions explicit and validated before relying on production failover paths.

constraints.title: Model Catalog Mode
constraints.description: Controls provider catalog behavior: "merge" keeps built-ins and overlays your custom providers, while "replace" uses only your configured providers. In "merge", matching provider IDs preserve non-empty agent models.json baseUrl values, while apiKey values are preserved only when the provider is not SecretRef-managed in current config/auth-profile context; SecretRef-managed providers refresh apiKey from current source markers, and matching model contextWindow/maxTokens use the higher value between explicit and implicit entries.

baseUrl.title: Model Provider Base URL
baseUrl.description: Base URL for the provider endpoint used to serve model requests for that provider entry. Use HTTPS endpoints and keep URLs environment-specific through config templating where needed.

apiKey.title: Model Provider API Key
apiKey.description: Provider credential used for API-key based authentication when the provider requires direct key auth. Use secret/env substitution and avoid storing real keys in committed config files.

auth.title: Model Provider Auth Mode
auth.description: Selects provider auth style: "api-key" for API key auth, "token" for bearer token auth, "oauth" for OAuth credentials, and "aws-sdk" for AWS credential resolution. Match this to your provider requirements.

api.title: Model Provider API Adapter
api.description: Provider API adapter selection controlling request/response compatibility handling for model calls. Use the adapter that matches your upstream provider protocol to avoid feature mismatch.

injectNumCtxForOpenAICompat.title: Model Provider Inject num_ctx (OpenAI Compat)
injectNumCtxForOpenAICompat.description: Controls whether OpenClaw injects `options.num_ctx` for Ollama providers configured with the OpenAI-compatible adapter (`openai-completions`). Default is true. Set false only if your proxy/upstream rejects unknown `options` payload fields.

headers.title: Model Provider Headers
headers.description: Static HTTP headers merged into provider requests for tenant routing, proxy auth, or custom gateway requirements. Use this sparingly and keep sensitive header values in secrets.

authHeader.title: Model Provider Authorization Header
authHeader.description: When true, credentials are sent via the HTTP Authorization header even if alternate auth is possible. Use this only when your provider or proxy explicitly requires Authorization forwarding.

headers.title: Model Provider Request Headers
headers.description: Extra headers merged into provider requests after default attribution and auth resolution.

token.title: Model Provider Request Bearer Token
token.description: Bearer token used when auth mode is authorization-bearer.

headerName.title: Model Provider Request Auth Header Name
headerName.description: Custom auth header name used when auth mode is header.

value.title: Model Provider Request Auth Header Value
value.description: Custom auth header value used when auth mode is header.

prefix.title: Model Provider Request Auth Header Prefix
prefix.description: Optional prefix prepended to request.auth.value when auth mode is header.

auth.title: Model Provider Request Auth Override
auth.description: Override provider request authentication behavior for this provider.

mode.title: Model Provider Request Proxy Mode
mode.description: Proxy override mode for model-provider requests: "env-proxy" or "explicit-proxy".

ca.title: Model Provider Request Proxy TLS CA
ca.description: Custom CA bundle used to verify the proxy TLS certificate chain.

cert.title: Model Provider Request Proxy TLS Cert
cert.description: Client TLS certificate presented to the proxy when mutual TLS is required.

key.title: Model Provider Request Proxy TLS Key
key.description: Private key paired with request.proxy.tls.cert for proxy mutual TLS.

passphrase.title: Model Provider Request Proxy TLS Passphrase
passphrase.description: Optional passphrase used to decrypt request.proxy.tls.key.

serverName.title: Model Provider Request Proxy TLS Server Name
serverName.description: Optional SNI/server-name override used when establishing TLS to the proxy.

insecureSkipVerify.title: Model Provider Request Proxy TLS Skip Verify
insecureSkipVerify.description: Skips proxy TLS certificate verification. Use only for controlled development environments.

tls.title: Model Provider Request Proxy TLS
tls.description: Optional TLS settings used when connecting to the configured proxy.

mode.title: Model Provider Request Proxy Mode
mode.description: Proxy override mode for model-provider requests: "env-proxy" or "explicit-proxy".

url.title: Model Provider Request Proxy URL
url.description: Explicit proxy URL used when request.proxy.mode is explicit-proxy. Credentials embedded in the URL are treated as sensitive and redacted from snapshots.

ca.title: Model Provider Request Proxy TLS CA
ca.description: Custom CA bundle used to verify the proxy TLS certificate chain.

cert.title: Model Provider Request Proxy TLS Cert
cert.description: Client TLS certificate presented to the proxy when mutual TLS is required.

key.title: Model Provider Request Proxy TLS Key
key.description: Private key paired with request.proxy.tls.cert for proxy mutual TLS.

passphrase.title: Model Provider Request Proxy TLS Passphrase
passphrase.description: Optional passphrase used to decrypt request.proxy.tls.key.

serverName.title: Model Provider Request Proxy TLS Server Name
serverName.description: Optional SNI/server-name override used when establishing TLS to the proxy.

insecureSkipVerify.title: Model Provider Request Proxy TLS Skip Verify
insecureSkipVerify.description: Skips proxy TLS certificate verification. Use only for controlled development environments.

tls.title: Model Provider Request Proxy TLS
tls.description: Optional TLS settings used when connecting to the configured proxy.

proxy.title: Model Provider Request Proxy
proxy.description: Optional proxy override for model-provider requests. Use "env-proxy" to honor environment proxy settings or "explicit-proxy" to route through a specific proxy URL.

ca.title: Model Provider Request TLS CA
ca.description: Custom CA bundle used to verify the upstream TLS certificate chain.

cert.title: Model Provider Request TLS Cert
cert.description: Client TLS certificate presented to the upstream endpoint when mutual TLS is required.

key.title: Model Provider Request TLS Key
key.description: Private key paired with request.tls.cert for upstream mutual TLS.

passphrase.title: Model Provider Request TLS Passphrase
passphrase.description: Optional passphrase used to decrypt request.tls.key.

serverName.title: Model Provider Request TLS Server Name
serverName.description: Optional SNI/server-name override used when establishing upstream TLS.

insecureSkipVerify.title: Model Provider Request TLS Skip Verify
insecureSkipVerify.description: Skips upstream TLS certificate verification. Use only for controlled development environments.

tls.title: Model Provider Request TLS
tls.description: Optional TLS settings used when connecting directly to the upstream model endpoint.

allowPrivateNetwork.title: Model Provider Request Allow Private Network
allowPrivateNetwork.description: When true, allow HTTPS to the model base URL when DNS resolves to private, CGNAT, or similar ranges, via the provider HTTP fetch guard (fetchWithSsrFGuard). OpenAI Responses WebSocket reuses request for headers/TLS but does not use that fetch SSRF path. Use only for operator-controlled self-hosted OpenAI-compatible endpoints (LAN, overlay, split DNS). Default is false.

request.title: Model Provider Request Overrides
request.description: Optional request overrides for model-provider requests, including extra headers, auth overrides, proxy routing, TLS client settings, and optional allowPrivateNetwork for trusted self-hosted endpoints. Use these only when your upstream or enterprise network path requires transport customization.

models.title: Model Provider Model List
models.description: Declared model list for a provider including identifiers, metadata, and optional compatibility/cost hints. Keep IDs exact to provider catalog values so selection and fallback resolve correctly.

constraints.title: Model Providers
constraints.description: Provider map keyed by provider ID containing connection/auth settings and concrete model definitions. Use stable provider keys so references from agents and tooling remain portable across environments.

constraints.title: Node Host
constraints.description: Node host controls for features exposed from this gateway node to other nodes or clients. Keep defaults unless you intentionally proxy local capabilities across your node network.

constraints.title: Node Browser Proxy
constraints.description: Groups browser-proxy settings for exposing local browser control through node routing. Enable only when remote node workflows need your local browser profiles.

constraints.title: Node Browser Proxy Allowed Profiles
constraints.description: Optional allowlist of browser profile names exposed through node proxy routing. Leave empty to preserve the default full profile surface, including profile create/delete routes. When set, OpenClaw enforces least-privilege profile access and blocks persistent profile create/delete through the proxy.

constraints.title: Node Browser Proxy Enabled
constraints.description: Expose the local browser control server through node proxy routing so remote clients can use this host's browser capabilities. Keep disabled unless remote automation explicitly depends on it.

constraints.title: Plugins
constraints.description: Plugin system controls for enabling extensions, constraining load scope, configuring entries, and tracking installs. Keep plugin policy explicit and least-privilege in production environments.

constraints.title: Plugin Allowlist
constraints.description: Optional allowlist of plugin IDs; when set, only listed plugins are eligible to load. Configured bundled chat channels can still activate their bundled plugin when the channel is explicitly enabled in config. Use this to enforce approved extension inventories in controlled environments.

constraints.title: Plugin Denylist
constraints.description: Optional denylist of plugin IDs that are blocked even if allowlists or paths include them. Use deny rules for emergency rollback and hard blocks on risky plugins.

constraints.title: Enable Plugins
constraints.description: Enable or disable plugin/extension loading globally during startup and config reload (default: true). Keep enabled only when extension capabilities are required by your deployment.

enabled.title: Plugin Enabled
enabled.description: Per-plugin enablement override for a specific entry, applied on top of global plugin policy (restart required). Use this to stage plugin rollout gradually across environments.

allowPromptInjection.title: Allow Prompt Injection Hooks
allowPromptInjection.description: Controls whether this plugin may mutate prompts through typed hooks. Set false to block `before_prompt_build` and ignore prompt-mutating fields from legacy `before_agent_start`, while preserving legacy `modelOverride` and `providerOverride` behavior.

hooks.title: Plugin Hook Policy
hooks.description: Per-plugin typed hook policy controls for core-enforced safety gates. Use this to constrain high-impact hook categories without disabling the entire plugin.

allowModelOverride.title: Allow Plugin Subagent Model Override
allowModelOverride.description: Explicitly allows this plugin to request provider/model overrides in background subagent runs. Keep false unless the plugin is trusted to steer model selection.

allowedModels.title: Plugin Subagent Allowed Models
allowedModels.description: Allowed override targets for trusted plugin subagent runs as canonical "provider/model" refs. Use "*" only when you intentionally allow any model.

subagent.title: Plugin Subagent Policy
subagent.description: Per-plugin subagent runtime controls for model override trust and allowlists. Keep this unset unless a plugin must explicitly steer subagent model selection.

config.title: Plugin Config
config.description: Plugin-defined configuration payload interpreted by that plugin's own schema and validation rules. Use only documented fields from the plugin to prevent ignored or invalid settings.

constraints.title: Plugin Entries
constraints.description: Per-plugin settings keyed by plugin ID including enablement and plugin-specific runtime configuration payloads. Use this for scoped plugin tuning without changing global loader policy.

constraints.title: Plugin Config
constraints.description: Plugin-defined configuration payload interpreted by that plugin's own schema and validation rules. Use only documented fields from the plugin to prevent ignored or invalid settings.

command.description: Command to run the MCP server

args.description: Arguments to pass to the command

env.description: Environment variables for the MCP server

constraints.title: Plugin Enabled
constraints.description: Per-plugin enablement override for a specific entry, applied on top of global plugin policy (restart required). Use this to stage plugin rollout gradually across environments.

constraints.title: Plugin Hook Policy
constraints.description: Per-plugin typed hook policy controls for core-enforced safety gates. Use this to constrain high-impact hook categories without disabling the entire plugin.

constraints.description: Controls whether this plugin may mutate prompts through typed hooks. Set false to block `before_prompt_build` and ignore prompt-mutating fields from legacy `before_agent_start`, while preserving legacy `modelOverride` and `providerOverride` behavior.

constraints.title: Plugin Subagent Policy
constraints.description: Per-plugin subagent runtime controls for model override trust and allowlists. Keep this unset unless a plugin must explicitly steer subagent model selection.

constraints.title: Allow Plugin Subagent Model Override
constraints.description: Explicitly allows this plugin to request provider/model overrides in background subagent runs. Keep false unless the plugin is trusted to steer model selection.

constraints.title: Plugin Subagent Allowed Models
constraints.description: Allowed override targets for trusted plugin subagent runs as canonical "provider/model" refs. Use "*" only when you intentionally allow any model.

constraints.title: Plugin Config
constraints.description: Plugin-defined configuration payload interpreted by that plugin's own schema and validation rules. Use only documented fields from the plugin to prevent ignored or invalid settings.

constraints.description: Per-plugin enablement override for a specific entry, applied on top of global plugin policy (restart required). Use this to stage plugin rollout gradually across environments.

constraints.title: Plugin Subagent Policy
constraints.description: Per-plugin subagent runtime controls for model override trust and allowlists. Keep this unset unless a plugin must explicitly steer subagent model selection.

constraints.title: Allow Plugin Subagent Model Override
constraints.description: Explicitly allows this plugin to request provider/model overrides in background subagent runs. Keep false unless the plugin is trusted to steer model selection.

constraints.description: Allowed override targets for trusted plugin subagent runs as canonical "provider/model" refs. Use "*" only when you intentionally allow any model.
