spec.title: Plugin Install Spec
spec.description: Original npm spec used for install (if source is npm).

sourcePath.title: Plugin Install Source Path
sourcePath.description: Original archive/path used for install (if any).

installPath.title: Plugin Install Path
installPath.description: Resolved install directory for the installed plugin bundle.

version.title: Plugin Install Version
version.description: Version recorded at install time (if available).

resolvedName.title: Plugin Resolved Package Name
resolvedName.description: Resolved npm package name from the fetched artifact.

resolvedVersion.title: Plugin Resolved Package Version
resolvedVersion.description: Resolved npm package version from the fetched artifact (useful for non-pinned specs).

resolvedSpec.title: Plugin Resolved Package Spec
resolvedSpec.description: Resolved exact npm spec (<name>@<version>) from the fetched artifact.

integrity.title: Plugin Resolved Integrity
integrity.description: Resolved npm dist integrity hash for the fetched artifact (if reported by npm).

shasum.title: Plugin Resolved Shasum
shasum.description: Resolved npm dist shasum for the fetched artifact (if reported by npm).

resolvedAt.title: Plugin Resolution Time
resolvedAt.description: ISO timestamp when npm package metadata was last resolved for this install record.

marketplaceName.title: Plugin Marketplace Name
marketplaceName.description: Marketplace display name recorded for marketplace-backed plugin installs (if available).

marketplaceSource.title: Plugin Marketplace Source
marketplaceSource.description: Original marketplace source used to resolve the install (for example a repo path or Git URL).

marketplacePlugin.title: Plugin Marketplace Plugin
marketplacePlugin.description: Plugin entry name inside the source marketplace, used for later updates.

constraints.title: Plugin Install Records
constraints.description: CLI-managed install metadata (used by `openclaw plugins update` to locate install sources).

constraints.title: Plugin Loader
constraints.description: Plugin loader configuration group for specifying filesystem paths where plugins are discovered. Keep load paths explicit and reviewed to avoid accidental untrusted extension loading.

constraints.title: Plugin Load Paths
constraints.description: Additional plugin files or directories scanned by the loader beyond built-in defaults. Use dedicated extension directories and avoid broad paths with unrelated executable content.

constraints.title: Plugin Slots
constraints.description: Selects which plugins own exclusive runtime slots such as memory so only one plugin provides that capability. Use explicit slot ownership to avoid overlapping providers with conflicting behavior.

constraints.title: Context Engine Plugin
constraints.description: Selects the active context engine plugin by id so one plugin provides context orchestration behavior.

constraints.title: Memory Plugin
constraints.description: Select the active memory plugin by id, or "none" to disable memory plugins.

constraints.title: Session
constraints.description: Global session routing, reset, delivery policy, and maintenance controls for conversation history behavior. Keep defaults unless you need stricter isolation, retention, or delivery constraints.

constraints.title: Session Agent-to-Agent
constraints.description: Groups controls for inter-agent session exchanges, including loop prevention limits on reply chaining. Keep defaults unless you run advanced agent-to-agent automation with strict turn caps.

constraints.title: Agent-to-Agent Ping-Pong Turns
constraints.description: Max reply-back turns between requester and target agents during agent-to-agent exchanges (0-5). Use lower values to hard-limit chatter loops and preserve predictable run completion.

constraints.title: DM Session Scope
constraints.description: DM session scoping: "main" keeps continuity, while "per-peer", "per-channel-peer", and "per-account-channel-peer" increase isolation. Use isolated modes for shared inboxes or multi-account deployments.

constraints.title: Session Identity Links
constraints.description: Maps canonical identities to provider-prefixed peer IDs so equivalent users resolve to one DM thread (example: telegram:123456). Use this when the same human appears across multiple channels or accounts.

constraints.title: Session Idle Minutes
constraints.description: Applies a legacy idle reset window in minutes for session reuse behavior across inactivity gaps. Use this only for compatibility and prefer structured reset policies under session.reset/session.resetByType.

constraints.title: Session Main Key
constraints.description: Overrides the canonical main session key used for continuity when dmScope or routing logic points to "main". Use a stable value only if you intentionally need custom session anchoring.

constraints.title: Session Maintenance
constraints.description: Automatic session-store maintenance controls for pruning age, entry caps, and file rotation behavior. Start in warn mode to observe impact, then enforce once thresholds are tuned.

constraints.title: Session Disk High-water Target
constraints.description: Target size after disk-budget cleanup (high-water mark). Defaults to 80% of maxDiskBytes; set explicitly for tighter reclaim behavior on constrained disks.

constraints.title: Session Max Disk Budget
constraints.description: Optional per-agent sessions-directory disk budget (for example `500mb`). Use this to cap session storage per agent; when exceeded, warn mode reports pressure and enforce mode performs oldest-first cleanup.

constraints.title: Session Max Entries
constraints.description: Caps total session entry count retained in the store to prevent unbounded growth over time. Use lower limits for constrained environments, or higher limits when longer history is required.

constraints.title: Session Maintenance Mode
constraints.description: Determines whether maintenance policies are only reported ("warn") or actively applied ("enforce"). Keep "warn" during rollout and switch to "enforce" after validating safe thresholds.

constraints.title: Session Prune After
constraints.description: Removes entries older than this duration (for example `30d` or `12h`) during maintenance passes. Use this as the primary age-retention control and align it with data retention policy.

constraints.title: Session Prune Days (Deprecated)
constraints.description: Deprecated age-retention field kept for compatibility with legacy configs using day counts. Use session.maintenance.pruneAfter instead so duration syntax and behavior are consistent.

constraints.title: Session Reset Archive Retention
constraints.description: Retention for reset transcript archives (`*.reset.<timestamp>`). Accepts a duration (for example `30d`), or `false` to disable cleanup. Defaults to pruneAfter so reset artifacts do not grow forever.

constraints.title: Session Rotate Size
constraints.description: Rotates the session store when file size exceeds a threshold such as `10mb` or `1gb`. Use this to bound single-file growth and keep backup/restore operations manageable.

constraints.title: Session Parent Fork Max Tokens
constraints.description: Maximum parent-session token count allowed for thread/session inheritance forking. If the parent exceeds this, OpenClaw starts a fresh thread session instead of forking; set 0 to disable this protection.

constraints.title: Session Reset Policy
constraints.description: Defines the default reset policy object used when no type-specific or channel-specific override applies. Set this first, then layer resetByType or resetByChannel only where behavior must differ.

constraints.title: Session Daily Reset Hour
constraints.description: Sets local-hour boundary (0-23) for daily reset mode so sessions roll over at predictable times. Use with mode=daily and align to operator timezone expectations for human-readable behavior.

constraints.title: Session Reset Idle Minutes
constraints.description: Sets inactivity window before reset for idle mode and can also act as secondary guard with daily mode. Use larger values to preserve continuity or smaller values for fresher short-lived threads.

constraints.title: Session Reset Mode
constraints.description: Selects reset strategy: "daily" resets at a configured hour and "idle" resets after inactivity windows. Keep one clear mode per policy to avoid surprising context turnover patterns.

constraints.title: Session Reset by Channel
constraints.description: Provides channel-specific reset overrides keyed by provider/channel id for fine-grained behavior control. Use this only when one channel needs exceptional reset behavior beyond type-level policies.

constraints.title: Session Reset by Chat Type
constraints.description: Overrides reset behavior by chat type (direct, group, thread) when defaults are not sufficient. Use this when group/thread traffic needs different reset cadence than direct messages.

constraints.title: Session Reset (Direct)
constraints.description: Defines reset policy for direct chats and supersedes the base session.reset configuration for that type. Use this as the canonical direct-message override instead of the legacy dm alias.

constraints.title: Session Reset (DM Deprecated Alias)
constraints.description: Deprecated alias for direct reset behavior kept for backward compatibility with older configs. Use session.resetByType.direct instead so future tooling and validation remain consistent.

constraints.title: Session Reset (Group)
constraints.description: Defines reset policy for group chat sessions where continuity and noise patterns differ from DMs. Use shorter idle windows for busy groups if context drift becomes a problem.

constraints.title: Session Reset (Thread)
constraints.description: Defines reset policy for thread-scoped sessions, including focused channel thread workflows. Use this when thread sessions should expire faster or slower than other chat types.

constraints.title: Session Reset Triggers
constraints.description: Lists message triggers that force a session reset when matched in inbound content. Use sparingly for explicit reset phrases so context is not dropped unexpectedly during normal conversation.

constraints.title: Session Scope
constraints.description: Sets base session grouping strategy: "per-sender" isolates by sender and "global" shares one session per channel context. Keep "per-sender" for safer multi-user behavior unless deliberate shared context is required.

constraints.title: Session Send Policy
constraints.description: Controls cross-session send permissions using allow/deny rules evaluated against channel, chatType, and key prefixes. Use this to fence where session tools can deliver messages in complex environments.

constraints.title: Session Send Policy Default Action
constraints.description: Sets fallback action when no sendPolicy rule matches: "allow" or "deny". Keep "allow" for simpler setups, or choose "deny" when you require explicit allow rules for every destination.

action.title: Session Send Rule Action
action.description: Defines rule decision as "allow" or "deny" when the corresponding match criteria are satisfied. Use deny-first ordering when enforcing strict boundaries with explicit allow exceptions.

channel.title: Session Send Rule Channel
channel.description: Matches rule application to a specific channel/provider id (for example discord, telegram, slack). Use this when one channel should permit or deny delivery independently of others.

chatType.title: Session Send Rule Chat Type
chatType.description: Matches rule application to chat type (direct, group, thread) so behavior varies by conversation form. Use this when DM and group destinations require different safety boundaries.

keyPrefix.title: Session Send Rule Key Prefix
keyPrefix.description: Matches a normalized session-key prefix after internal key normalization steps in policy consumers. Use this for general prefix controls, and prefer rawKeyPrefix when exact full-key matching is required.

rawKeyPrefix.title: Session Send Rule Raw Key Prefix
rawKeyPrefix.description: Matches the raw, unnormalized session-key prefix for exact full-key policy targeting. Use this when normalized keyPrefix is too broad and you need agent-prefixed or transport-specific precision.

match.title: Session Send Rule Match
match.description: Defines optional rule match conditions that can combine channel, chatType, and key-prefix constraints. Keep matches narrow so policy intent stays readable and debugging remains straightforward.

constraints.title: Session Send Policy Rules
constraints.description: Ordered allow/deny rules evaluated before the default action, for example `{ action: "deny", match: { channel: "discord" } }`. Put most specific rules first so broad rules do not shadow exceptions.

constraints.title: Session Store Path
constraints.description: Sets the session storage file path used to persist session records across restarts. Use an explicit path only when you need custom disk layout, backup routing, or mounted-volume storage.

constraints.title: Session Thread Bindings
constraints.description: Shared defaults for thread-bound session routing behavior across providers that support thread focus workflows. Configure global defaults here and override per channel only when behavior differs.

constraints.title: Thread Binding Enabled
constraints.description: Global master switch for thread-bound session routing features and focused thread delivery behavior. Keep enabled for modern thread workflows unless you need to disable thread binding globally.

constraints.title: Thread Binding Idle Timeout (hours)
constraints.description: Default inactivity window in hours for thread-bound sessions across providers/channels (0 disables idle auto-unfocus). Default: 24.

constraints.title: Thread Binding Max Age (hours)
constraints.description: Optional hard max age in hours for thread-bound sessions across providers/channels (0 disables hard cap). Default: 0.

constraints.title: Session Typing Interval (seconds)
constraints.description: Controls interval for repeated typing indicators while replies are being prepared in typing-capable channels. Increase to reduce chatty updates or decrease for more active typing feedback.

constraints.title: Session Typing Mode
constraints.description: Controls typing behavior timing: "never", "instant", "thinking", or "message" based emission points. Keep conservative modes in high-volume channels to avoid unnecessary typing noise.

constraints.title: Watch Skills
constraints.description: Enable filesystem watching for skill-definition changes so updates can be applied without full process restart. Keep enabled in development workflows and disable in immutable production images.

constraints.title: Skills Watch Debounce (ms)
constraints.description: Debounce window in milliseconds for coalescing rapid skill file changes before reload logic runs. Increase to reduce reload churn on frequent writes, or lower for faster edit feedback.

constraints.title: Talk
constraints.description: Talk-mode voice synthesis settings for voice identity, model selection, output format, and interruption behavior. Use this section to tune human-facing voice UX while controlling latency and cost.

constraints.title: Talk Interrupt on Speech
constraints.description: If true (default), stop assistant speech when the user starts speaking in Talk mode. Keep enabled for conversational turn-taking.

constraints.title: Talk Active Provider
constraints.description: Active Talk provider id (for example "acme-speech").

apiKey.title: Talk Provider API Key
apiKey.description: Provider API key for Talk mode.

additionalProperties.title: Talk Provider Config
additionalProperties.description: Provider-owned Talk config fields for the matching provider id.

constraints.title: Talk Provider Settings
constraints.description: Provider-specific Talk settings keyed by provider id. During migration, prefer this over legacy talk.* keys.

constraints.title: Talk Silence Timeout (ms)
constraints.description: Milliseconds of user silence before Talk mode finalizes and sends the current transcript. Leave unset to keep the platform default pause window (700 ms on macOS and Android, 900 ms on iOS).

constraints.title: Tools
constraints.description: Global tool access policy and capability configuration across web, exec, media, messaging, and elevated surfaces. Use this section to constrain risky capabilities before broad rollout.

constraints.title: Agent-to-Agent Tool Access
constraints.description: Policy for allowing agent-to-agent tool calls and constraining which target agents can be reached. Keep disabled or tightly scoped unless cross-agent orchestration is intentionally enabled.

constraints.title: Agent-to-Agent Target Allowlist
constraints.description: Allowlist of target agent IDs permitted for agent_to_agent calls when orchestration is enabled. Use explicit allowlists to avoid uncontrolled cross-agent call graphs.

constraints.title: Enable Agent-to-Agent Tool
constraints.description: Enables the agent_to_agent tool surface so one agent can invoke another agent at runtime. Keep off in simple deployments and enable only when orchestration value outweighs complexity.

constraints.title: Tool Allowlist
constraints.description: Absolute tool allowlist that replaces profile-derived defaults for strict environments. Use this only when you intentionally run a tightly curated subset of tool capabilities.

constraints.title: Tool Allowlist Additions
constraints.description: Extra tool allowlist entries merged on top of the selected tool profile and default policy. Keep this list small and explicit so audits can quickly identify intentional policy exceptions.

constraints.title: Tool Policy by Provider
constraints.description: Per-provider tool allow/deny overrides keyed by channel/provider ID to tailor capabilities by surface. Use this when one provider needs stricter controls than global tool policy.

constraints.title: Tool Denylist
constraints.description: Global tool denylist that blocks listed tools even when profile or provider rules would allow them. Use deny rules for emergency lockouts and long-term defense-in-depth.

constraints.title: Elevated Tool Access
constraints.description: Elevated tool access controls for privileged command surfaces that should only be reachable from trusted senders. Keep disabled unless operator workflows explicitly require elevated actions.

constraints.title: Elevated Tool Allow Rules
constraints.description: Sender allow rules for elevated tools, usually keyed by channel/provider identity formats. Use narrow, explicit identities so elevated commands cannot be triggered by unintended users.

constraints.title: Enable Elevated Tool Access
constraints.description: Enables elevated tool execution path when sender and policy checks pass. Keep disabled in public/shared channels and enable only for trusted owner-operated contexts.

constraints.title: Exec Tool
constraints.description: Exec-tool policy grouping for shell execution host, security mode, approval behavior, and runtime bindings. Keep conservative defaults in production and tighten elevated execution paths.

constraints.title: apply_patch Model Allowlist
constraints.description: Optional allowlist of model ids (e.g. "gpt-5.4" or "openai/gpt-5.4").

constraints.title: Enable apply_patch
constraints.description: Enable or disable apply_patch for OpenAI and OpenAI Codex models when allowed by tool policy (default: true).

constraints.title: apply_patch Workspace-Only
constraints.description: Restrict apply_patch paths to the workspace directory (default: true). Set false to allow writing outside the workspace (dangerous).

constraints.title: Exec Ask
constraints.description: Approval strategy for when exec commands require human confirmation before running. Use stricter ask behavior in shared channels and lower-friction settings in private operator contexts.

constraints.title: Exec Target
constraints.description: Selects execution target strategy for shell commands. Use "auto" for runtime-aware behavior (sandbox when available, otherwise gateway), or pin sandbox/gateway/node explicitly when you need a fixed surface.

constraints.title: Exec Node Binding
constraints.description: Node binding configuration for exec tooling when command execution is delegated through connected nodes. Use explicit node binding only when multi-node routing is required.

constraints.title: Exec Notify On Exit
constraints.description: When true (default), backgrounded exec sessions on exit and node exec lifecycle events enqueue a system event and request a heartbeat.

constraints.title: Exec Notify On Empty Success
constraints.description: When true, successful backgrounded exec exits with empty output still enqueue a completion system event (default: false).

constraints.title: Exec PATH Prepend
constraints.description: Directories to prepend to PATH for exec runs (gateway/sandbox).

constraints.title: Exec Safe Bin Profiles
constraints.description: Optional per-binary safe-bin profiles (positional limits + allowed/denied flags).

constraints.title: Exec Safe Bin Trusted Dirs
constraints.description: Additional explicit directories trusted for safe-bin path checks (PATH entries are never auto-trusted).

constraints.title: Exec Safe Bins
constraints.description: Allow stdin-only safe binaries to run without explicit allowlist entries.

constraints.title: Exec Security
constraints.description: Execution security posture selector controlling sandbox/approval expectations for command execution. Keep strict security mode for untrusted prompts and relax only for trusted operator workflows.

constraints.title: Require Inline-Eval Approval
constraints.description: Require explicit approval for interpreter inline-eval forms such as `python -c`, `node -e`, `ruby -e`, or `osascript -e`. Prevents silent allowlist reuse and downgrades allow-always to ask-each-time for those forms.

constraints.title: Experimental Tools
constraints.description: Experimental built-in tool flags. Keep these off by default and enable only when you are intentionally testing a preview surface.

constraints.title: Enable Structured Plan Tool
constraints.description: Enable the experimental structured `update_plan` tool for non-trivial multi-step work tracking. Leave this off unless you explicitly want the tool outside strict-agentic embedded Pi runs.

constraints.title: Workspace-only FS tools
constraints.description: Restrict filesystem tools (read/write/edit/apply_patch) to the workspace directory (default: false).

constraints.title: Enable Link Understanding
constraints.description: Enable automatic link understanding pre-processing so URLs can be summarized before agent reasoning. Keep enabled for richer context, and disable when strict minimal processing is required.

constraints.title: Link Understanding Max Links
constraints.description: Maximum number of links expanded per turn during link understanding. Use lower values to control latency/cost in chatty threads and higher values when multi-link context is critical.

constraints.title: Link Understanding Models
constraints.description: Preferred model list for link understanding tasks, evaluated in order as fallbacks when supported. Use lightweight models first for routine summarization and heavier models only when needed.

constraints.title: Link Understanding Scope
constraints.description: Controls when link understanding runs relative to conversation context and message type. Keep scope conservative to avoid unnecessary fetches on messages where links are not actionable.

constraints.title: Link Understanding Timeout (sec)
constraints.description: Per-link understanding timeout budget in seconds before unresolved links are skipped. Keep this bounded to avoid long stalls when external sites are slow or unreachable.

constraints.title: Tool-loop Critical Threshold
constraints.description: Critical threshold for repetitive patterns when detector is enabled (default: 20).

constraints.title: Tool-loop Generic Repeat Detection
constraints.description: Enable generic repeated same-tool/same-params loop detection (default: true).

constraints.title: Tool-loop Poll No-Progress Detection
constraints.description: Enable known poll tool no-progress loop detection (default: true).

constraints.title: Tool-loop Ping-Pong Detection
constraints.description: Enable ping-pong loop detection (default: true).

constraints.title: Tool-loop Detection
constraints.description: Enable repetitive tool-call loop detection and backoff safety checks (default: false).

constraints.title: Tool-loop Global Circuit Breaker Threshold
constraints.description: Global no-progress breaker threshold (default: 30).

constraints.title: Tool-loop History Size
constraints.description: Tool history window size for loop detection (default: 30).

constraints.title: Unknown-tool Loop Threshold
constraints.description: Block repeated calls to the same unavailable tool after this many misses (default: 10).

constraints.title: Tool-loop Warning Threshold
constraints.description: Warning threshold for repetitive patterns when detector is enabled (default: 10).

constraints.title: Async Media Completion Direct Send
constraints.description: Enable direct channel sends for completed async music/video generation tasks instead of relying on the requester session wake path. Default off so detached media completion keeps the legacy model-delivery flow unless you opt in.

constraints.title: Audio Understanding Attachment Policy
constraints.description: Attachment policy for audio inputs indicating which uploaded files are eligible for audio processing. Keep restrictive defaults in mixed-content channels to avoid unintended audio workloads.

constraints.title: Transcript Echo Format
constraints.description: Format string for the echoed transcript message. Use `{transcript}` as a placeholder for the transcribed text. Default: '📝 "{transcript}"'.

constraints.title: Echo Transcript to Chat
constraints.description: Echo the audio transcript back to the originating chat before agent processing. When enabled, users immediately see what was heard from their voice note, helping them verify transcription accuracy before the agent acts on it. Default: false.

constraints.title: Enable Audio Understanding
constraints.description: Enable audio understanding so voice notes or audio clips can be transcribed/summarized for agent context. Disable when audio ingestion is outside policy or unnecessary for your workflows.

constraints.title: Audio Understanding Language
constraints.description: Preferred language hint for audio understanding/transcription when provider support is available. Set this to improve recognition accuracy for known primary languages.

constraints.title: Audio Understanding Max Bytes
constraints.description: Maximum accepted audio payload size in bytes before processing is rejected or clipped by policy. Set this based on expected recording length and upstream provider limits.

constraints.title: Audio Understanding Max Chars
constraints.description: Maximum characters retained from audio understanding output to prevent oversized transcript injection. Increase for long-form dictation, or lower to keep conversational turns compact.

constraints.title: Audio Understanding Models
constraints.description: Ordered model preferences specifically for audio understanding, used before shared media model fallback. Choose models optimized for transcription quality in your primary language/domain.

constraints.title: Audio Understanding Prompt
constraints.description: Instruction template guiding audio understanding output style, such as concise summary versus near-verbatim transcript. Keep wording consistent so downstream automations can rely on output format.

constraints.title: Audio Request Overrides
constraints.description: Low-level HTTP request overrides for audio providers, including custom headers, auth, proxy routing, and TLS client settings. Use this for proxy-backed or self-hosted transcription endpoints when plain baseUrl/apiKey fields are not enough.

mode.title: Audio Request Auth Mode
mode.description: Auth override mode for audio requests: "provider-default" keeps the normal provider auth, "authorization-bearer" forces an Authorization bearer token, and "header" sends a custom header/value pair.

mode.title: Audio Request Auth Mode
mode.description: Auth override mode for audio requests: "provider-default" keeps the normal provider auth, "authorization-bearer" forces an Authorization bearer token, and "header" sends a custom header/value pair.

token.title: Audio Request Bearer Token
token.description: Bearer token used when audio request auth.mode is authorization-bearer. Keep this in secret storage rather than inline config.

mode.title: Audio Request Auth Mode
mode.description: Auth override mode for audio requests: "provider-default" keeps the normal provider auth, "authorization-bearer" forces an Authorization bearer token, and "header" sends a custom header/value pair.

headerName.title: Audio Request Auth Header Name
headerName.description: Header name used when audio request auth.mode is header. Match the exact upstream expectation, such as x-api-key or authorization.

value.title: Audio Request Auth Header Value
value.description: Header value used when audio request auth.mode is header. Keep secrets in env-backed values and avoid duplicating provider-default auth unnecessarily.

prefix.title: Audio Request Auth Header Prefix
prefix.description: Optional prefix prepended to the custom auth header value, such as Bearer. Leave unset when the upstream expects the raw credential only.

constraints.title: Audio Request Auth Override
constraints.description: Optional auth override for audio provider requests. Use this when the upstream expects a non-default bearer token or custom auth header shape.

constraints.title: Audio Request Headers
constraints.description: Additional HTTP headers merged into audio provider requests after provider defaults. Use this for tenant routing or proxy integration headers, and keep secrets in env-backed values.

mode.title: Audio Request Proxy Mode
mode.description: Proxy mode for audio requests: "env-proxy" uses environment proxy settings, while "explicit-proxy" uses the configured proxy URL only for this request path.

tls.title: Audio Request Proxy TLS
tls.description: TLS settings applied when connecting to the configured audio proxy, such as custom CA trust for an internal proxy gateway.

mode.title: Audio Request Proxy Mode
mode.description: Proxy mode for audio requests: "env-proxy" uses environment proxy settings, while "explicit-proxy" uses the configured proxy URL only for this request path.

url.title: Audio Request Proxy URL
url.description: Explicit proxy URL for audio provider traffic when proxy.mode is explicit-proxy. Keep credentials out of inline URLs when possible and prefer secret-backed env injection.

tls.title: Audio Request Proxy TLS
tls.description: TLS settings applied when connecting to the configured audio proxy, such as custom CA trust for an internal proxy gateway.

constraints.title: Audio Request Proxy
constraints.description: Proxy transport override for audio requests. Use env-proxy to respect process proxy settings, or explicit-proxy to force a dedicated proxy URL for this provider path.

constraints.title: Audio Request TLS
constraints.description: Direct TLS client settings for audio provider requests, including custom CA trust, client certs, or SNI overrides for managed gateways and internal endpoints.

constraints.title: Audio Understanding Scope
constraints.description: Scope selector for when audio understanding runs across inbound messages and attachments. Keep focused scopes in high-volume channels to reduce cost and avoid accidental transcription.

constraints.title: Audio Understanding Timeout (sec)
constraints.description: Timeout in seconds for audio understanding execution before the operation is cancelled. Use longer timeouts for long recordings and tighter ones for interactive chat responsiveness.

constraints.title: Media Understanding Concurrency
constraints.description: Maximum number of concurrent media understanding operations per turn across image, audio, and video tasks. Lower this in resource-constrained deployments to prevent CPU/network saturation.

constraints.title: Image Understanding Attachment Policy
constraints.description: Attachment handling policy for image inputs, including which message attachments qualify for image analysis. Use restrictive settings in untrusted channels to reduce unexpected processing.

constraints.title: Enable Image Understanding
constraints.description: Enable image understanding so attached or referenced images can be interpreted into textual context. Disable if you need text-only operation or want to avoid image-processing cost.

constraints.title: Image Understanding Max Bytes
constraints.description: Maximum accepted image payload size in bytes before the item is skipped or truncated by policy. Keep limits realistic for your provider caps and infrastructure bandwidth.

constraints.title: Image Understanding Max Chars
constraints.description: Maximum characters returned from image understanding output after model response normalization. Use tighter limits to reduce prompt bloat and larger limits for detail-heavy OCR tasks.

constraints.title: Image Understanding Models
constraints.description: Ordered model preferences specifically for image understanding when you want to override shared media models. Put the most reliable multimodal model first to reduce fallback attempts.

constraints.title: Image Understanding Prompt
constraints.description: Instruction template used for image understanding requests to shape extraction style and detail level. Keep prompts deterministic so outputs stay consistent across turns and channels.

constraints.title: Image Understanding Scope
constraints.description: Scope selector for when image understanding is attempted (for example only explicit requests versus broader auto-detection). Keep narrow scope in busy channels to control token and API spend.

constraints.title: Image Understanding Timeout (sec)
constraints.description: Timeout in seconds for each image understanding request before it is aborted. Increase for high-resolution analysis and lower it for latency-sensitive operator workflows.

constraints.title: Media Understanding Shared Models
constraints.description: Shared fallback model list used by media understanding tools when modality-specific model lists are not set. Keep this aligned with available multimodal providers to avoid runtime fallback churn.

constraints.title: Video Understanding Attachment Policy
constraints.description: Attachment eligibility policy for video analysis, defining which message files can trigger video processing. Keep this explicit in shared channels to prevent accidental large media workloads.

constraints.title: Enable Video Understanding
constraints.description: Enable video understanding so clips can be summarized into text for downstream reasoning and responses. Disable when processing video is out of policy or too expensive for your deployment.

constraints.title: Video Understanding Max Bytes
constraints.description: Maximum accepted video payload size in bytes before policy rejection or trimming occurs. Tune this to provider and infrastructure limits to avoid repeated timeout/failure loops.

constraints.title: Video Understanding Max Chars
constraints.description: Maximum characters retained from video understanding output to control prompt growth. Raise for dense scene descriptions and lower when concise summaries are preferred.

constraints.title: Video Understanding Models
constraints.description: Ordered model preferences specifically for video understanding before shared media fallback applies. Prioritize models with strong multimodal video support to minimize degraded summaries.

constraints.title: Video Understanding Prompt
constraints.description: Instruction template for video understanding describing desired summary granularity and focus areas. Keep this stable so output quality remains predictable across model/provider fallbacks.

constraints.title: Video Understanding Scope
constraints.description: Scope selector controlling when video understanding is attempted across incoming events. Narrow scope in noisy channels, and broaden only where video interpretation is core to workflow.

constraints.title: Video Understanding Timeout (sec)
constraints.description: Timeout in seconds for each video understanding request before cancellation. Use conservative values in interactive channels and longer values for offline or batch-heavy processing.

constraints.title: Allow Cross-Context Messaging
constraints.description: Legacy override: allow cross-context sends across all providers.

constraints.title: Enable Message Broadcast
constraints.description: Enable broadcast action (default: true).

constraints.title: Allow Cross-Context (Across Providers)
constraints.description: Allow sends across different providers (default: false).

constraints.title: Allow Cross-Context (Same Provider)
constraints.description: Allow sends to other channels within the same provider (default: true).

constraints.title: Cross-Context Marker
constraints.description: Add a visible origin marker when sending cross-context (default: true).

constraints.title: Cross-Context Marker Prefix
constraints.description: Text prefix for cross-context markers (supports "{channel}").

constraints.title: Cross-Context Marker Suffix
constraints.description: Text suffix for cross-context markers (supports "{channel}").

constraints.title: Tool Profile
constraints.description: Global tool profile name used to select a predefined tool policy baseline before applying allow/deny overrides. Use this for consistent environment posture across agents and keep profile names stable.

constraints.title: Sandbox Tool Policy
constraints.description: Tool policy wrapper for sandboxed agent executions so sandbox runs can have distinct capability boundaries. Use this to enforce stronger safety in sandbox contexts.

constraints.title: Sandbox Tool Allow/Deny Policy
constraints.description: Allow/deny tool policy applied when agents run in sandboxed execution environments. Keep policies minimal so sandbox tasks cannot escalate into unnecessary external actions.

constraints.title: Session Tools Visibility
constraints.description: Controls which sessions can be targeted by sessions_list/sessions_history/sessions_send. ("tree" default = current session + spawned subagent sessions; "self" = only current; "agent" = any session in the current agent id; "all" = any session; cross-agent still requires tools.agentToAgent).

constraints.title: Subagent Tool Policy
constraints.description: Tool policy wrapper for spawned subagents to restrict or expand tool availability compared to parent defaults. Use this to keep delegated agent capabilities scoped to task intent.

constraints.title: Subagent Tool Allow/Deny Policy
constraints.description: Allow/deny tool policy applied to spawned subagent runtimes for per-subagent hardening. Keep this narrower than parent scope when subagents run semi-autonomous workflows.

constraints.title: Web Tools
constraints.description: Web-tool policy grouping for search/fetch providers, limits, and fallback behavior tuning. Keep enabled settings aligned with API key availability and outbound networking policy.

constraints.title: Web Fetch Cache TTL (min)
constraints.description: Cache TTL in minutes for web_fetch results.

constraints.title: Enable Web Fetch Tool
constraints.description: Enable the web_fetch tool (lightweight HTTP fetch).

constraints.title: Web Fetch Max Chars
constraints.description: Max characters returned by web_fetch (truncated).

constraints.title: Web Fetch Hard Max Chars
constraints.description: Hard cap for web_fetch maxChars (applies to config and tool calls).

constraints.title: Web Fetch Max Redirects
constraints.description: Maximum redirects allowed for web_fetch (default: 3).

constraints.title: Web Fetch Max Download Size (bytes)
constraints.description: Max download size before truncation.

constraints.title: Web Fetch Provider
constraints.description: Web fetch fallback provider id.

constraints.title: Web Fetch Readability Extraction
constraints.description: Use Readability to extract main content from HTML (fallbacks to basic HTML cleanup).

constraints.title: Web Fetch SSRF Policy
constraints.description: Scoped SSRF policy overrides for web_fetch. Keep this narrow and opt in only for known local-network proxy environments.

constraints.title: Web Fetch Allow RFC 2544 Benchmark Range
constraints.description: Allow RFC 2544 benchmark-range IPs (198.18.0.0/15) for fake-IP proxy compatibility such as Clash or Surge.

constraints.title: Web Fetch Timeout (sec)
constraints.description: Timeout in seconds for web_fetch requests.

constraints.title: Web Fetch User-Agent
constraints.description: Override User-Agent header for web_fetch requests.

constraints.title: Web Search Cache TTL (min)
constraints.description: Cache TTL in minutes for web_search results.

constraints.title: Enable Web Search Tool
constraints.description: Enable managed web_search and optional Codex-native search for eligible models.

constraints.title: Web Search Max Results
constraints.description: Number of results to return (1-10).

constraints.title: Codex Allowed Domains
constraints.description: Optional domain allowlist passed to the native Codex web_search tool.

constraints.title: Codex Search Context Size
constraints.description: Native Codex search context size hint: "low", "medium", or "high".

constraints.title: Enable Native Codex Web Search
constraints.description: Enable native Codex web search for Codex-capable models.

constraints.title: Codex Web Search Mode
constraints.description: Native Codex web search mode: "cached" (default) or "live".

constraints.title: Web Search Provider
constraints.description: Search provider id. Auto-detected from available API keys if omitted.

constraints.title: Web Search Timeout (sec)
constraints.description: Timeout in seconds for web_search requests.

constraints.title: UI
constraints.description: UI presentation settings for accenting and assistant identity shown in control surfaces. Use this for branding and readability customization without changing runtime behavior.

constraints.title: Assistant Appearance
constraints.description: Assistant display identity settings for name and avatar shown in UI surfaces. Keep these values aligned with your operator-facing persona and support expectations.

constraints.title: Assistant Avatar
constraints.description: Assistant avatar image source used in UI surfaces (URL, path, or data URI depending on runtime support). Use trusted assets and consistent branding dimensions for clean rendering.

constraints.title: Assistant Name
constraints.description: Display name shown for the assistant in UI views, chat chrome, and status contexts. Keep this stable so operators can reliably identify which assistant persona is active.

constraints.title: Accent Color
constraints.description: Primary accent color used by UI surfaces for emphasis, badges, and visual identity cues. Use high-contrast values that remain readable across light/dark themes.

constraints.title: Updates
constraints.description: Update-channel and startup-check behavior for keeping OpenClaw runtime versions current. Use conservative channels in production and more experimental channels only in controlled environments.

constraints.title: Auto Update Beta Check Interval (hours)
constraints.description: How often beta-channel checks run in hours (default: 1).

constraints.title: Auto Update Enabled
constraints.description: Enable background auto-update for package installs (default: false).

constraints.title: Auto Update Stable Delay (hours)
constraints.description: Minimum delay before stable-channel auto-apply starts (default: 6).

constraints.title: Auto Update Stable Jitter (hours)
constraints.description: Extra stable-channel rollout spread window in hours (default: 12).

constraints.title: Update Channel
constraints.description: Update channel for git + npm installs ("stable", "beta", or "dev").

constraints.title: Update Check on Start
constraints.description: Check for npm updates when the gateway starts (default: true).

constraints.title: Web Channel
constraints.description: Web channel runtime settings for heartbeat and reconnect behavior when operating web-based chat surfaces. Use reconnect values tuned to your network reliability profile and expected uptime needs.

constraints.title: Web Channel Enabled
constraints.description: Enables the web channel runtime and related websocket lifecycle behavior. Keep disabled when web chat is unused to reduce active connection management overhead.

constraints.title: Web Channel Heartbeat Interval (sec)
constraints.description: Heartbeat interval in seconds for web channel connectivity and liveness maintenance. Use shorter intervals for faster detection, or longer intervals to reduce keepalive chatter.

constraints.title: Web Channel Reconnect Policy
constraints.description: Reconnect backoff policy for web channel reconnect attempts after transport failure. Keep bounded retries and jitter tuned to avoid thundering-herd reconnect behavior.

constraints.title: Web Reconnect Backoff Factor
constraints.description: Exponential backoff multiplier used between reconnect attempts in web channel retry loops. Keep factor above 1 and tune with jitter for stable large-fleet reconnect behavior.

constraints.title: Web Reconnect Initial Delay (ms)
constraints.description: Initial reconnect delay in milliseconds before the first retry after disconnection. Use modest delays to recover quickly without immediate retry storms.

constraints.title: Web Reconnect Jitter
constraints.description: Randomization factor (0-1) applied to reconnect delays to desynchronize clients after outage events. Keep non-zero jitter in multi-client deployments to reduce synchronized spikes.

constraints.title: Web Reconnect Max Attempts
constraints.description: Maximum reconnect attempts before giving up for the current failure sequence (0 means no retries). Use finite caps for controlled failure handling in automation-sensitive environments.

constraints.title: Web Reconnect Max Delay (ms)
constraints.description: Maximum reconnect backoff cap in milliseconds to bound retry delay growth over repeated failures. Use a reasonable cap so recovery remains timely after prolonged outages.

constraints.title: Setup Wizard State
constraints.description: Setup wizard state tracking fields that record the most recent guided setup run details. Keep these fields for observability and troubleshooting of setup flows across upgrades.

constraints.title: Wizard Last Run Timestamp
constraints.description: ISO timestamp for when the setup wizard most recently completed on this host. Use this to confirm setup recency during support and operational audits.

constraints.title: Wizard Last Run Command
constraints.description: Command invocation recorded for the latest wizard run to preserve execution context. Use this to reproduce setup steps when verifying setup regressions.

constraints.title: Wizard Last Run Commit
constraints.description: Source commit identifier recorded for the last wizard execution in development builds. Use this to correlate setup behavior with exact source state during debugging.

constraints.title: Wizard Last Run Mode
constraints.description: Wizard execution mode recorded as "local" or "remote" for the most recent setup flow. Use this to understand whether setup targeted direct local runtime or remote gateway topology.

constraints.title: Wizard Last Run Version
constraints.description: OpenClaw version recorded at the time of the most recent wizard run on this config. Use this when diagnosing behavior differences across version-to-version setup changes.
